exploits/windows/fileformat/ms14_060_san.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in Windows Object Linking and Embedding (OLE)
          allowing arbitrary code execution, publicly known as "Sandworm". Platforms such as Windows
          Vista SP2 all the way to Windows 8, Windows Server 2008 and 2012 are known to be
          vulnerable. However, based on our testing, the most reliable setup is on Windows platforms
          running Office 2013 and Office 2010 SP2. And please keep in mind that some other setups such
          as using Office 2010 SP1 might be less stable, and sometimes may end up with a crash due to
          a failure in the CPackage::CreateTempFileName function.

          This module will generate three files: an INF, a GIF, and a PPSX file. You are required to
          set up a SMB or Samba 3 server and host the INF and GIF there. Systems such as Ubuntu or an
          older version of Windows (such as XP) work best for this because they require little
          configuration to get going. The PPSX file is what you should send to your target.

          In detail, the vulnerability has to do with how the Object Packager 2 component
          (packager.dll) handles an INF file that contains malicious registry changes, which may be
          leveraged for code execution. First of all, Packager does not load the INF file directly.
          As an attacker, you can trick it to load your INF anyway by embedding the file path as
          a remote share in an OLE object. The packager will then treat it as a type of media file,
          and load it with the packager!CPackage::OLE2MPlayerReadFromStream function, which will
          download it with a CopyFileW call, save it in a temp folder, and pass that information for
          later. The exploit will do this loading process twice: first for a fake gif file that's
          actually the payload, and the second for the INF file.

          The packager will also look at each OLE object's XML Presentation Command, specifically the
          type and cmd property. In the exploit, "verb" media command type is used, and this triggers
          the packager!CPackage::DoVerb function. Also, "-3" is used as the fake gif file's cmd
          property, and "3" is used for the INF. When the cmd is "-3", DoVerb will bail. But when "3"
          is used (again, for the INF file), it will cause the packager to try to find appropriate
          handler for it, which will end up with C:\Windows\System32\infDefaultInstall.exe, and that
          will install/run the malicious INF file, and finally give us arbitrary code execution.
exploits/windows/fileformat/ms14_060_san.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/ms14_064_pac.description:

          SP2 all the way to Windows 8, Windows Server 2008 and 2012 are known to be vulnerable.
          However, based on our testing, the most reliable setup is on Windows platforms running
          Office 2013 and Office 2010 SP2. Please keep in mind that some other setups such as

exploits/windows/fileformat/mswin_tiff_o.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in Microsoft's Tagged Image File Format.
          It was originally discovered in the wild, targeting Windows XP and Windows Server 2003
          users running Microsoft Office, specifically in the Middle East and South Asia region.

          The flaw is due to a DWORD value extracted from the TIFF file that is embedded as a
          drawing in Microsoft Office, and how it gets calculated with user-controlled inputs,
          and stored in the EAX register. The 32-bit register will run out of storage space to
          represent the large value, which ends up being 0, but it still gets pushed as a
          dwBytes argument (size) for a HeapAlloc call. The HeapAlloc function will allocate a
          chunk anyway with size 0, and the address of this chunk is used as the destination buffer
          of a memcpy function, where the source buffer is the EXIF data (an extended image format
          supported by TIFF), and is also user-controlled. A function pointer in the chunk returned
          by HeapAlloc will end up being overwritten by the memcpy function, and then later used
          in OGL!GdipCreatePath. By successfully controlling this function pointer, and the
          memory layout using ActiveX, it is possible to gain arbitrary code execution under the
          context of the user.
targets: Windows XP SP3 with Office Standard 2010
exploits/windows/fileformat/mswin_tiff_o.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/theme_dll_hi.name: Themebleed- Windows 11 Themes Arbitrary Code Execution CVE-2023-38146
exploits/windows/fileformat/theme_dll_hi.description:
When an unpatched Windows 11 host loads a theme file referencing an msstyles file, Windows loads the
          msstyles file, and if that file's PACKME_VERSION is `999`, it then attempts to load an accompanying dll
          file ending in `_vrf.dll` Before loading that file, it verifies that the file is signed.  It does this by
          opening the file for reading and verifying the signature before opening the file for execution.
          Because this action is performed in two discrete operations, it opens the procedure for a time of check to
          time of use vulnerability.  By embedding a UNC file path to an SMB server we control, the SMB server can
          serve a legitimate, signed dll when queried for the read, but then serve a different file of the same name
          when the host intends to load/execute the dll.

          This exploit bypasses DEP & ASLR, and works on XP, Vista & Windows 7.
targets: Windows XP/Vista/Win7/... Generic DEP & ASLR Bypass
exploits/windows/fileformat/videospirit_.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/visio_dxf_bo.name: Microsoft Office Visio VISIODWG.DLL DXF File Handling Vulnerability
exploits/windows/fileformat/visio_dxf_bo.description:
This module exploits a stack based overflow vulnerability in the handling
          of the DXF files by Microsoft Visio 2002. Revisions prior to the release of
          the MS bulletin MS10-028 are vulnerable. The overflow occurs when the application
          is used to import a specially crafted DXF file, while parsing the HEADER section
          of the DXF file.

          To trigger the vulnerability an attacker must convince someone to insert a
          specially crafted DXF file to a new document, go to 'Insert' -> 'CAD Drawing'
targets: Visio 2002 English on Windows XP SP3 Spanish
exploits/windows/fileformat/visio_dxf_bo.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/visiwave_vwr.name: VisiWave VWR File Parsing Vulnerability
exploits/windows/fileformat/visiwave_vwr.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in VisiWave's Site Survey Report application.
          When processing .VWR files, VisiWaveReport.exe attempts to match a valid pointer based on the 'Type'
          property (valid ones include 'Properties', 'TitlePage', 'Details', 'Graph', 'Table', 'Text',
          'Image'), but if a match isn't found, the function that's supposed to handle this routine
          ends up returning the input as a pointer, and later used in a CALL DWORD PTR [EDX+10]
          instruction.  This allows attackers to overwrite it with any arbitrary value, and results code
          execution.  A patch is available at visiwave.com; the fix is done by XORing the return value as
          null if no match is found, and then it is validated before use.

          NOTE: During installation, the application will register two file handles, VWS and VWR, which allows a
          victim user to 'double click' the malicious VWR file and execute code.  This module was also built
          to bypass ASLR and DEP.
exploits/windows/fileformat/visiwave_vwr.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/vlc_mkv.name: VLC Media Player MKV Use After Free
exploits/windows/fileformat/vlc_mkv.description:
This module exploits a use after free vulnerability in
          VideoLAN VLC =< 2.2.8. The vulnerability exists in the parsing of
          MKV files and affects both 32 bits and 64 bits.

          In order to exploit this, this module will generate two files:
          The first .mkv file contains the main vulnerability and heap spray,
          the second .mkv file is required in order to take the vulnerable code
          path and should be placed under the same directory as the .mkv file.

          This module has been tested against VLC v2.2.8. Tested with payloads
          windows/exec, windows/x64/exec, windows/shell/reverse_tcp,
          windows/x64/shell/reverse_tcp. Meterpreter payloads if used can
          cause the application to crash instead.
exploits/windows/fileformat/vlc_mkv.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/vlc_modplug_.name: VideoLAN VLC ModPlug ReadS3M Stack Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/fileformat/vlc_modplug_.description:
This module exploits an input validation error in libmod_plugin as
          included with VideoLAN VLC 1.1.8. All versions prior to version 1.1.9
          are affected. By creating a malicious S3M file, a remote attacker
          could execute arbitrary code.

          Although other products that bundle libmodplug may be vulnerable, this
          module was only tested against VLC.

          NOTE: As of July 1st, 2010, VLC now calls SetProcessDEPPoly to
          permanently enable NX support on machines that support it. As such,
          this module is capable of bypassing DEP, but not ASLR.
exploits/windows/fileformat/vlc_modplug_.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/vlc_realtext.name: VLC Media Player RealText Subtitle Overflow
exploits/windows/fileformat/vlc_realtext.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow vulnerability in
          VideoLAN VLC < 0.9.6. The vulnerability exists in the parsing of
          RealText subtitle files.

          In order to exploit this, this module will generate two files:
          The .mp4 file is used to trick your victim into running. The .rt file
          is the actual malicious file that triggers the vulnerability, which
          should be placed under the same directory as the .mp4 file.
targets: VLC 0.9.4 on Windows XP SP3 / Windows 7 SP1
exploits/windows/fileformat/vlc_realtext.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/vlc_smb_uri.name: VideoLAN Client (VLC) Win32 smb:// URI Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/fileformat/vlc_smb_uri.description:
This module exploits a stack-based buffer overflow in the Win32AddConnection
          function of the VideoLAN VLC media player. Versions 0.9.9 through 1.0.1 are
          reportedly affected.

          This vulnerability is only present in Win32 builds of VLC.

          This payload was found to work with the windows/exec and
          windows/meterpreter/reverse_tcp payloads. However, the
          windows/meterpreter/reverse_ord_tcp was found not to work.
exploits/windows/fileformat/vlc_smb_uri.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/vlc_webm.name: VideoLAN VLC MKV Memory Corruption
exploits/windows/fileformat/vlc_webm.description:
This module exploits an input validation error in VideoLAN VLC
          < 1.1.7.  By creating a malicious MKV or WebM file, a remote attacker
          could execute arbitrary code.

          NOTE: As of July 1st, 2010, VLC now calls SetProcessDEPPoly to
          permanently enable NX support on machines that support it.
exploits/windows/fileformat/vlc_webm.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/vuplayer_cue.name: VUPlayer CUE Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/fileformat/vuplayer_cue.description:
This module exploits a stack based overflow in VUPlayer <= 2.49. When
          the application is used to open a specially crafted cue file, a buffer is overwritten allowing
          for the execution of arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/fileformat/vuplayer_cue.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/vuplayer_m3u.name: VUPlayer M3U Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/fileformat/vuplayer_m3u.description:
This module exploits a stack over flow in VUPlayer <= 2.49. When
          the application is used to open a specially crafted m3u file, an buffer is overwritten allowing
          for the execution of arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/fileformat/vuplayer_m3u.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/watermark_ma.name: Watermark Master Buffer Overflow (SEH)
exploits/windows/fileformat/watermark_ma.description:
This module exploits a stack based buffer overflow in Watermark Master 2.2.23 when
          processing a specially crafted .WCF file. This vulnerability could be
          exploited by a remote attacker to execute arbitrary code on the target
          machine by enticing a user of Watermark Master to open a malicious .WCF file.
exploits/windows/fileformat/watermark_ma.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/winamp_maki_.name: Winamp MAKI Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/fileformat/winamp_maki_.description:
This module exploits a stack based buffer overflow in Winamp 5.55. The flaw
          exists in the gen_ff.dll and occurs while parsing a specially crafted MAKI file,
          where memmove is used in an insecure way with user controlled data.

          To exploit the vulnerability the attacker must convince the victim to install the
          generated mcvcore.maki file in the "scripts" directory of the default "Bento" skin,
          or generate a new skin using the crafted mcvcore.maki file. The module has been
          tested successfully on Windows XP SP3 and Windows 7 SP1.
targets: Winamp 5.55 / Windows XP SP3 / Windows 7 SP1
exploits/windows/fileformat/winamp_maki_.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/windows_regi.name: Malicious Windows Registration Entries (.reg) File
exploits/windows/fileformat/windows_regi.description:
This module creates a Windows Registration Entries (.reg) file which
          adds the specified payload to the Windows Registry. The payload runs
          upon Windows login for the current user. If the user has elevated
          privileges when opening the file, the payload will run upon login
          when any user logs in.

          The user will receive a warning prompt to confirm Registry changes
          when opening the file.
exploits/windows/fileformat/windows_regi.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/windows_scri.name: Malicious Windows Script Host JScript (.js) File
exploits/windows/fileformat/windows_scri.description: This module creates a Windows Script Host (WSH) JScript (.js) file.
exploits/windows/fileformat/windows_scri.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/windows_scri.name: Malicious Windows Script Host VBScript (.vbs) File
exploits/windows/fileformat/windows_scri.description: This module creates a Windows Script Host (WSH) VBScript (.vbs) file.
exploits/windows/fileformat/windows_scri.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/windows_scri.name: Malicious Windows Script Host Script File (.wsf)
exploits/windows/fileformat/windows_scri.description: This module creates a Windows Script Host (WSH) Windows Script File (.wsf).
exploits/windows/fileformat/windows_scri.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/winrar_ace.name: RARLAB WinRAR ACE Format Input Validation Remote Code Execution
exploits/windows/fileformat/winrar_ace.description:
In WinRAR versions prior to and including 5.61, there is path traversal vulnerability
          when crafting the filename field of the ACE format (in UNACEV2.dll). When the filename
          field is manipulated with specific patterns, the destination (extraction) folder is
          ignored, thus treating the filename as an absolute path. This module will attempt to
          extract a payload to the startup folder of the current user. It is limited such that
          we can only go back one folder. Therefore, for this exploit to work properly, the user
          must extract the supplied RAR file from one folder within the user profile folder
          (e.g. Desktop or Downloads). User restart is required to gain a shell.
exploits/windows/fileformat/winrar_ace.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/winrar_cve_2.name: WinRAR CVE-2023-38831 Exploit
exploits/windows/fileformat/winrar_cve_2.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability in WinRAR (CVE-2023-38831). When a user opens a crafted RAR file and its
          embedded document, the decoy document is executed, leading to code execution.
exploits/windows/fileformat/winrar_cve_2.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/winrar_name_.name: WinRAR Filename Spoofing
exploits/windows/fileformat/winrar_name_.description:
This module abuses a filename spoofing vulnerability in WinRAR. The vulnerability exists
          when opening ZIP files. The file names showed in WinRAR when opening a ZIP file come from
          the central directory, but the file names used to extract and open contents come from the
          Local File Header. This inconsistency allows to spoof file names when opening ZIP files
          with WinRAR, which can be abused to execute arbitrary code, as exploited in the wild in
          March 2014
exploits/windows/fileformat/winrar_name_.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/wireshark_mp.name: Wireshark wiretap/mpeg.c Stack Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/fileformat/wireshark_mp.description:
This module triggers a stack buffer overflow in Wireshark <= 1.8.12/1.10.5
          by generating a malicious file.
exploits/windows/fileformat/wireshark_mp.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/wireshark_pa.name: Wireshark packet-dect.c Stack Buffer Overflow (local)
exploits/windows/fileformat/wireshark_pa.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Wireshark <= 1.4.4
          When opening a malicious .pcap file in Wireshark, a stack buffer occurs,
          resulting in arbitrary code execution.

          Note: To exploit the vulnerability remotely with Scapy: sendp(rdpcap("file")).
exploits/windows/fileformat/wireshark_pa.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/wm_downloade.name: WM Downloader 3.1.2.2 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/fileformat/wm_downloade.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in WM Downloader v3.1.2.2. When
          the application is used to import a specially crafted m3u file, a buffer overflow occurs
          allowing arbitrary code execution.
exploits/windows/fileformat/wm_downloade.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/word_msdtjs_.name: Microsoft Office Word MSDTJS
exploits/windows/fileformat/word_msdtjs_.description:
This module generates a malicious Microsoft Word document that when loaded, will leverage the remote template
          feature to fetch an `HTML` document and then use the `ms-msdt` scheme to execute `PowerShell` code.
exploits/windows/fileformat/word_msdtjs_.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/word_mshtml_.name: Microsoft Office Word Malicious MSHTML RCE
exploits/windows/fileformat/word_mshtml_.description:
This module creates a malicious docx file that when opened in Word on a vulnerable Windows
          system will lead to code execution. This vulnerability exists because an attacker can
          craft a malicious ActiveX control to be used by a Microsoft Office document that hosts
          the browser rendering engine.
exploits/windows/fileformat/word_mshtml_.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/xenorate_xpl.name: Xenorate 2.50 (.xpl) Universal Local Buffer Overflow (SEH)
exploits/windows/fileformat/xenorate_xpl.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Xenorate 2.50
          by creating a specially crafted xpl file.
exploits/windows/fileformat/xenorate_xpl.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/xion_m3u_seh.name: Xion Audio Player 1.0.126 Unicode Stack Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/fileformat/xion_m3u_seh.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Xion Audio Player prior to version
          1.0.126. The vulnerability is triggered when opening a malformed M3U file that
          contains an overly long string. This results in overwriting a
          structured exception handler record.
targets: Xion Audio Player v1.0.126 XP Universal
exploits/windows/fileformat/xion_m3u_seh.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/xradio_xrl_s.name: xRadio 0.95b Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/fileformat/xradio_xrl_s.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in xRadio 0.95b.
          Using the application to import a specially crafted xrl file,
          a buffer overflow occurs allowing arbitrary code execution.
exploits/windows/fileformat/xradio_xrl_s.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/zahir_enterp.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Zahir Enterprise Plus version 6 build 10b and below.
          The vulnerability is triggered when opening a CSV file containing CR/LF and overly long string characters
          via Import from other File. This results in overwriting a structured exception handler record.
exploits/windows/fileformat/zahir_enterp.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/fileformat/zinfaudiopla.name: Zinf Audio Player 2.2.1 (PLS File) Stack Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/fileformat/zinfaudiopla.description:
This module exploits a stack-based buffer overflow in the Zinf Audio Player 2.2.1.
          An attacker must send the file to victim and the victim must open the file.
          Alternatively it may be possible to execute code remotely via an embedded
          PLS file within a browser, when the PLS extension is registered to Zinf.
          This functionality has not been tested in this module.
exploits/windows/fileformat/zinfaudiopla.reason: Module path indicates client-side file format or social engineering exploit

exploits/windows/emc/alphastor_agent.name: EMC AlphaStor Agent Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/emc/alphastor_agent.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in EMC AlphaStor 3.1.
          By sending a specially crafted message, an attacker may
          be able to execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/emc/alphastor_agent.reason: EMC AlphaStor is third-party enterprise backup software not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/emc/alphastor_device_ma.name: EMC AlphaStor Device Manager Opcode 0x75 Command Injection
exploits/windows/emc/alphastor_device_ma.description:
This module exploits a flaw within the Device Manager (rrobtd.exe). When parsing the 0x75
          command, the process does not properly filter user supplied input allowing for arbitrary
          command injection. This module has been tested successfully on EMC AlphaStor 4.0 build 116
          with Windows 2003 SP2 and Windows 2008 R2.
exploits/windows/emc/alphastor_device_ma.reason: Targets EMC AlphaStor backup software, not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/emc/networker_format_st.name: EMC Networker Format String
exploits/windows/emc/networker_format_st.description:
This module exploits a format string vulnerability in the lg_sprintf function
          as implemented in liblocal.dll on EMC Networker products. This module exploits the
          vulnerability by using a specially crafted RPC call to the program number 0x5F3DD,
          version 0x02, and procedure 0x06. This module has been tested successfully on EMC
          Networker 7.6 SP3 on Windows XP SP3 and Windows 2003 SP2 (DEP bypass).
exploits/windows/emc/networker_format_st.reason: Targets EMC Networker, not a default Windows service, so unlikely to be present in a standard VM lab.

exploits/windows/emc/replication_manager.name: EMC Replication Manager Command Execution
exploits/windows/emc/replication_manager.description:
This module exploits a remote command-injection vulnerability in EMC Replication Manager
          client (irccd.exe). By sending a specially crafted message invoking RunProgram function an
          attacker may be able to execute arbitrary commands with SYSTEM privileges. Affected
          products are EMC Replication Manager < 5.3. This module has been successfully tested
          against EMC Replication Manager 5.2.1 on XP/W2003. EMC Networker Module for Microsoft
          Applications 2.1 and 2.2 may be vulnerable too although this module have not been tested
          against these products.
targets: EMC Replication Manager 5.2.1 / Windows Native Payload
exploits/windows/emc/replication_manager.reason: Targets EMC Replication Manager, a third-party backup product not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/email/ms07_017_ani_load.name: Windows ANI LoadAniIcon() Chunk Size Stack Buffer Overflow (SMTP)
exploits/windows/email/ms07_017_ani_load.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow vulnerability in the
          LoadAniIcon() function of USER32.dll. The flaw is triggered
          through Outlook Express by using the CURSOR style sheet
          directive to load a malicious .ANI file.

          This vulnerability was discovered by Alexander Sotirov of Determina
          and was rediscovered, in the wild, by McAfee.
exploits/windows/email/ms07_017_ani_load.reason: Requires victim to open a malicious email in Outlook Express, triggering client-side ANI parsing.

exploits/windows/email/ms10_045_outlook_.name: Outlook ATTACH_BY_REF_ONLY File Execution
exploits/windows/email/ms10_045_outlook_.description:
It has been discovered that certain e-mail message cause Outlook to create Windows
          shortcut-like attachments or messages within Outlook. Through specially crafted TNEF
          streams with certain MAPI attachment properties, it is possible to set a path name
          to files to be executed. When a user double clicks on such an attachment or message,
          Outlook will proceed to execute the file that is set by the path name value. These
          files can be local files, but also files stored remotely (on a file share, for example)
          can be used. Exploitation is limited by the fact that it is not possible for attackers
          to supply command line options.
exploits/windows/email/ms10_045_outlook_.reason: Requires victim to open a malicious email attachment in Outlook, which is user interaction.

exploits/windows/email/ms10_045_outlook_.name: Outlook ATTACH_BY_REF_RESOLVE File Execution
exploits/windows/email/ms10_045_outlook_.description:
It has been discovered that certain e-mail message cause Outlook to create Windows
          shortcut-like attachments or messages within Outlook. Through specially crafted TNEF
          streams with certain MAPI attachment properties, it is possible to set a path name
          to files to be executed. When a user double clicks on such an attachment or message,
          Outlook will proceed to execute the file that is set by the path name value. These
          files can be local files, but also file stored remotely for example on a file share.
          Exploitation is limited by the fact that its is not possible for attackers to supply
          command line options.
exploits/windows/email/ms10_045_outlook_.reason: Requires victim to double-click a malicious attachment in Outlook

exploits/windows/dcerpc/cve_2021_1675_pr.name: Print Spooler Remote DLL Injection
exploits/windows/dcerpc/cve_2021_1675_pr.description:
The print spooler service can be abused by an authenticated remote attacker to load a DLL through a crafted
          DCERPC request, resulting in remote code execution as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM. This module uses the MS-RPRN
          vector which requires the Print Spooler service to be running.
exploits/windows/dcerpc/cve_2021_1675_pr.reason: Requires valid domain or local credentials to coerce the spooler to load the attacker DLL, so it is lateral-movement not an initial foothold.

exploits/windows/dcerpc/ms03_026_dcom.name: MS03-026 Microsoft RPC DCOM Interface Overflow
exploits/windows/dcerpc/ms03_026_dcom.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the RPCSS service, this vulnerability
          was originally found by the Last Stage of Delirium research group and has been
          widely exploited ever since. This module can exploit the English versions of
          Windows NT 4.0 SP3-6a, Windows 2000, Windows XP, and Windows 2003 all in one request :)
exploits/windows/dcerpc/ms03_026_dcom.reason: Exploits the RPCSS DCOM service over TCP 135/445 without credentials or user interaction.

exploits/windows/dcerpc/ms05_017_msmq.name: MS05-017 Microsoft Message Queueing Service Path Overflow
exploits/windows/dcerpc/ms05_017_msmq.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the RPC interface
          to the Microsoft Message Queueing service. The offset to the
          return address changes based on the length of the system
          hostname, so this must be provided via the 'HNAME' option.
          Much thanks to snort.org and Jean-Baptiste Marchand's
          excellent MSRPC website.

exploits/windows/dcerpc/ms07_029_msdns_z.name: MS07-029 Microsoft DNS RPC Service extractQuotedChar() Overflow (TCP)
exploits/windows/dcerpc/ms07_029_msdns_z.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the RPC interface
          of the Microsoft DNS service. The vulnerability is triggered
          when a long zone name parameter is supplied that contains
          escaped octal strings. This module is capable of bypassing NX/DEP
          protection on Windows 2003 SP1/SP2.
exploits/windows/dcerpc/ms07_029_msdns_z.reason: Exploits a network-facing DNS RPC service for unauthenticated RCE with no user interaction.

exploits/windows/dcerpc/ms07_065_msmq.name: MS07-065 Microsoft Message Queueing Service DNS Name Path Overflow
exploits/windows/dcerpc/ms07_065_msmq.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the RPC interface
          to the Microsoft Message Queueing service. This exploit requires
          the target system to have been configured with a DNS name and
          for that name to be supplied in the 'DNAME' option. This name does
          not need to be served by a valid DNS server, only configured on
          the target machine.
exploits/windows/dcerpc/ms07_065_msmq.reason: Exploits a network-facing RPC service (MSMQ) to gain remote code execution without credentials or user interaction.

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_cooltype_.name: Adobe CoolType SING Table "uniqueName" Stack Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_cooltype_.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability in the Smart INdependent Glyplets (SING) table
          handling within versions 8.2.4 and 9.3.4 of Adobe Reader. Prior versions are
          assumed to be vulnerable as well.
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_cooltype_.reason: Requires victim to open malicious PDF in browser/Reader, so it is client-side not initial remote foothold.

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_avm.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in the ActiveX component of Adobe Flash Player
          before 12.0.0.43. By supplying a specially crafted swf file it is possible to trigger an
          integer underflow in several avm2 instructions, which can be turned into remote code
          execution under the context of the user, as exploited in the wild in February 2014. This
          module has been tested successfully with Adobe Flash Player 11.7.700.202 on Windows XP
          SP3, Windows 7 SP1 and Adobe Flash Player 11.3.372.94 on Windows 8 even when it includes
          rop chains for several Flash 11 versions, as exploited in the wild.
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_avm.reason: Requires victim to open malicious SWF in browser (client-side interaction).

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_cas.name: Adobe Flash Player casi32 Integer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_cas.description:
This module exploits an integer overflow in Adobe Flash Player. The vulnerability occurs in
          the casi32 method, where an integer overflow occurs if a ByteArray of length 0 is setup as
          domainMemory for the current application domain. This module has been tested successfully
          on Windows 7 SP1 (32-bit), IE 8 to IE 11 and Flash 15.0.0.167.
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_cas.reason: Requires victim to browse to attacker-controlled page (client-side interaction).

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_cop.name: Adobe Flash Player copyPixelsToByteArray Method Integer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_cop.description:
This module exploits an integer overflow in Adobe Flash Player. The vulnerability occurs
          in the copyPixelsToByteArray method from the BitmapData object. The position field of the
          destination ByteArray can be used to cause an integer overflow and write contents out of
          the ByteArray buffer. This module has been tested successfully on:
          * Windows 7 SP1 (32-bit), IE 8 to IE 11 and Flash 14.0.0.176, 14.0.0.145, and 14.0.0.125.
          * Windows 7 SP1 (32-bit), Firefox 38.0.5 and Adobe Flash 14.0.0.179.
          * Windows 8.1, Firefox 38.0.5 and Adobe Flash 14.0.0.179.
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_cop.reason: Module requires victim to visit a malicious URL in browser

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_dom.name: Adobe Flash Player domainMemory ByteArray Use After Free
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_dom.description:
This module exploits a use-after-free vulnerability in Adobe Flash Player. The
          vulnerability occurs when the ByteArray assigned to the current ApplicationDomain
          is freed from an ActionScript worker, when forcing a reallocation by copying more
          contents than the original capacity, but Flash forgets to update the domainMemory
          pointer, leading to a use-after-free situation when the main worker references the
          domainMemory again. This module has been tested successfully on Windows 7 SP1
          (32-bit), IE 8 and IE11 with Flash 17.0.0.134.
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_dom.reason: Requires victim to browse to attacker-controlled page (client-side interaction).

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_fil.description:
This module exploits a type confusion vulnerability found in the ActiveX
          component of Adobe Flash Player. This vulnerability was found exploited
          in the wild in November 2013. This module has been tested successfully
          on IE 6 to IE 10 with Flash 11.7, 11.8 and 11.9 prior to 11.9.900.170
          over Windows XP SP3 and Windows 7 SP1.
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_fil.reason: Requires victim to browse to attacker-controlled page (client-side interaction).

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_mp4.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in Adobe Flash
          Player.  By supplying a corrupt .mp4 file loaded by Flash, it
          is possible to gain arbitrary remote code execution under the
          context of the user.

          This vulnerability has been exploited in the wild as part of
          the "Iran's Oil and Nuclear Situation.doc" e-mail attack.
          According to the advisory, 10.3.183.15 and 11.x before
          11.1.102.62 are affected.

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_otf.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in the ActiveX component of Adobe
          Flash Player before 11.3.300.271. By supplying a specially crafted .otf font file
          with a large nTables value in the 'kern' header, it is possible to trigger an
          integer overflow, which results in remote code execution under the context of the
          user.  This vulnerability has also been exploited in the wild in limited targeted
          attacks.  Please note in order to ensure reliability, the exploit is forced to
          modify your URIPATH parameter to less than 3 characters, which may cause possible
          URIPATH collisions.
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_otf.reason: Requires victim to browse to attacker URL and open malicious Flash content

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_pcr.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in Adobe Flash Player. A compilation logic error
          in the PCRE engine, specifically in the handling of the \c escape sequence when followed by
          a multi-byte UTF8 character, allows arbitrary execution of PCRE bytecode.
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_pcr.reason: Requires victim to open malicious Flash content in browser (client-side interaction).

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_reg.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in the ActiveX component of Adobe
          Flash Player before 11.5.502.149. By supplying a specially crafted swf file
          with special regex value, it is possible to trigger a memory corruption, which
          results in remote code execution under the context of the user, as exploited in
          the wild in February 2013. This module has been tested successfully with Adobe
          Flash Player 11.5 before 11.5.502.149 on Windows XP SP3 and Windows 7 SP1 before
          MS13-063, since it takes advantage of a predictable SharedUserData in order to
          leak ntdll and bypass ASLR.
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_reg.reason: Requires victim to open malicious SWF in browser (client-side interaction).

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_rtm.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in Adobe Flash
          Player.  By supplying a corrupt AMF0 "_error" response, it
          is possible to gain arbitrary remote code execution under
          the context of the user.

          This vulnerability has been exploited in the wild as part of
          the "World Uyghur Congress Invitation.doc" e-mail attack.
          According to the advisory, 10.3.183.19 and 11.x before
          11.2.202.235 are affected.

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_sps.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in Adobe Flash Player's Flash10u.ocx
          component.  When processing a MP4 file (specifically the Sequence Parameter Set),
          Flash will see if pic_order_cnt_type is equal to 1, which sets the
          num_ref_frames_in_pic_order_cnt_cycle field, and then blindly copies data in
          offset_for_ref_frame on the stack, which allows arbitrary remote code execution
          under the context of the user.  Numerous reports also indicate that this
          vulnerability has been exploited in the wild.
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_sps.reason: Requires victim to open malicious Flash content in browser (client-side interaction).

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_unc.name: Adobe Flash Player UncompressViaZlibVariant Uninitialized Memory
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_unc.description:
This module exploits an uninitialized memory vulnerability in Adobe Flash Player. The
          vulnerability occurs in the ByteArray::UncompressViaZlibVariant method, which fails
          to initialize allocated memory. When using a correct memory layout this vulnerability
          leads to a ByteArray object corruption, which can be abused to access and corrupt memory.
          This module has been tested successfully on Windows 7 SP1 (32-bit), IE 8 and IE11 with
          Flash 15.0.0.189.
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_unc.reason: Requires victim to visit a malicious page in Internet Explorer, so it is client-side.

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_wor.name: Adobe Flash Player ByteArray With Workers Use After Free
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_wor.description:
This module exploits a use-after-free vulnerability in Adobe Flash Player. The
          vulnerability occurs when the ByteArray assigned to the current ApplicationDomain
          is freed from an ActionScript worker, which can fill the memory and notify the main
          thread to corrupt the new contents. This module has been tested successfully on
          Windows 7 SP1 (32-bit), IE 8 to IE 11 and Flash 16.0.0.296.
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flash_wor.reason: Requires victim to browse to attacker page, so it is client-side not initial foothold.

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flashplay.name: Adobe Flash Player AVM Verification Logic Array Indexing Code Execution
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flashplay.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability in Adobe Flash Player versions 10.3.181.23
          and earlier. This issue is caused by a failure in the ActionScript3 AVM2 verification
          logic. This results in unsafe JIT(Just-In-Time) code being executed. This is the same
          vulnerability that was used for attacks against Korean based organizations.

          Specifically, this issue occurs when indexing an array using an arbitrary value,
          memory can be referenced and later executed. Taking advantage of this issue does not rely
          on heap spraying as the vulnerability can also be used for information leakage.

          Currently this exploit works for IE6, IE7, IE8, Firefox 10.2 and likely several
          other browsers under multiple Windows platforms. This exploit bypasses ASLR/DEP and
          is very reliable.
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flashplay.reason: Module requires victim to visit a malicious URL in browser

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flashplay.name: Adobe Flash Player AVM Bytecode Verification Vulnerability
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flashplay.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability in Adobe Flash Player versions 10.2.152.33
          and earlier. This issue is caused by a failure in the ActionScript3 AVM2 verification
          logic. This results in unsafe JIT(Just-In-Time) code being executed.  This is the same
          vulnerability that was used for the RSA attack in March 2011.

          Specifically, this issue results in uninitialized memory being referenced and later
          executed. Taking advantage of this issue relies on heap spraying and controlling the
          uninitialized memory.

          Currently this exploit works for IE6, IE7, and Firefox 3.6 and likely several
          other browsers. DEP does catch the exploit and causes it to fail. Due to the nature
          of the uninitialized memory its fairly difficult to get around this restriction.
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flashplay.reason: Module requires victim to visit a malicious URL in browser

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flashplay.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability in Adobe Flash Player that was discovered,
          and has been exploited actively in the wild.  By embedding a specially crafted .swf
          file, Adobe Flash crashes due to an invalid use of an object type, which allows
          attackers to overwrite a pointer in memory, and results arbitrary code execution.
          Please note for IE 8 targets, Java Runtime Environment must be available on the
          victim machine in order to work properly.

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flashplay.name: Adobe Flash Player "newfunction" Invalid Pointer Use
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flashplay.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability in the DoABC tag handling within
          versions 9.x and 10.0 of Adobe Flash Player. Adobe Reader and Acrobat are also
          vulnerable, as are any other applications that may embed Flash player.

          Arbitrary code execution is achieved by embedding a specially crafted Flash
          movie into a PDF document. An AcroJS heap spray is used in order to ensure
          that the memory used by the invalid pointer issue is controlled.

          NOTE: This module uses a similar DEP bypass method to that used within the
          adobe_libtiff module. This method is unlikely to work across various
          Windows versions due a hardcoded syscall number.

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flatedeco.name: Adobe FlateDecode Stream Predictor 02 Integer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flatedeco.description:
This module exploits an integer overflow vulnerability in Adobe Reader and Adobe
          Acrobat Professional versions before 9.2.
targets: Adobe Reader Windows Universal (JS Heap Spray)
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_flatedeco.reason: Requires victim to open malicious PDF in browser/Reader, so it is client-side not initial remote foothold.

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_geticon.name: Adobe Collab.getIcon() Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_geticon.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in Adobe Reader and Adobe Acrobat.
          Affected versions include < 7.1.1, < 8.1.3, and < 9.1. By creating a specially
          crafted pdf that a contains malformed Collab.getIcon() call, an attacker may
          be able to execute arbitrary code.

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_jbig2deco.name: Adobe JBIG2Decode Heap Corruption
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_jbig2deco.description:
This module exploits a heap-based pointer corruption flaw in Adobe Reader 9.0.0 and earlier.
          This module relies upon javascript for the heap spray.
targets: Adobe Reader v9.0.0 (Windows XP SP3 English)
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_jbig2deco.reason: Victim must open a malicious PDF in Adobe Reader, requiring user interaction.

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_media_new.name: Adobe Doc.media.newPlayer Use After Free Vulnerability
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_media_new.description:
This module exploits a use after free vulnerability in Adobe Reader and Adobe Acrobat
          Professional versions up to and including 9.2.
targets: Adobe Reader Windows English (JS Heap Spray)

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_shockwave.name: Adobe Shockwave rcsL Memory Corruption
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_shockwave.description:
This module exploits a weakness in the Adobe Shockwave player's handling of
          Director movies (.DIR). A memory corruption vulnerability occurs through an undocumented
          rcsL chunk.

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_toolbutto.description:
This module exploits an use after free condition on Adobe Reader versions 11.0.2, 10.1.6
          and 9.5.4 and prior. The vulnerability exists while handling the ToolButton object, where
          the cEnable callback can be used to early free the object memory. Later use of the object
          allows triggering the use after free condition. This module has been tested successfully
          on Adobe Reader 11.0.2 and 10.0.4, with IE and Windows XP SP3, as exploited in the wild in
          November, 2013. At the moment, this module doesn't support Adobe Reader 9 targets; in order
          to exploit Adobe Reader 9 the fileformat version of the exploit can be used.
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_toolbutto.reason: Requires victim to open malicious PDF in browser, so it is client-side not initial remote foothold.

exploits/windows/browser/adobe_utilprint.name: Adobe util.printf() Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/adobe_utilprint.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in Adobe Reader and Adobe Acrobat Professional
          < 8.1.3. By creating a specially crafted pdf that a contains malformed util.printf()
          entry, an attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code.
targets: Adobe Reader v8.1.2 (Windows XP SP3 English)

exploits/windows/browser/advantech_webac.name: Advantech WebAccess dvs.ocx GetColor Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/advantech_webac.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow vulnerability in Advantec WebAccess. The
          vulnerability exists in the dvs.ocx ActiveX control, where a dangerous call to
          sprintf can be reached with user controlled data through the GetColor function.
          This module has been tested successfully on Windows XP SP3 with IE6 and Windows
          7 SP1 with IE8 and IE 9.
exploits/windows/browser/advantech_webac.reason: Requires victim to visit a malicious page in IE, making it client-side only.

exploits/windows/browser/aim_goaway.name: AOL Instant Messenger goaway Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/aim_goaway.description:
This module exploits a flaw in the handling of AOL Instant
          Messenger's 'goaway' URI handler.  An attacker can execute
          arbitrary code by supplying an overly sized buffer as the
          'message' parameter.  This issue is known to affect AOL Instant
          Messenger 5.5.
exploits/windows/browser/aim_goaway.reason: Requires victim to click on a crafted AIM goaway:// link, so it is client-side interaction.

exploits/windows/browser/aladdin_choosef.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in Aladdin Knowledge System's
          ActiveX component.  By supplying a long string of data to the ChooseFilePath()
          function, a buffer overflow occurs, which may result in remote code execution
          under the context of the user.
exploits/windows/browser/aladdin_choosef.reason: Requires victim to visit a malicious page or open an HTML file, making it client-side.

exploits/windows/browser/amaya_bdo.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the Amaya v11 Browser.
          By sending an overly long string to the "bdo"
          tag, an attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/browser/amaya_bdo.reason: Module requires victim to visit a malicious URL in browser

exploits/windows/browser/aol_ampx_conver.name: AOL Radio AmpX ActiveX Control ConvertFile() Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/aol_ampx_conver.description:
This module exploits a stack-based buffer overflow in AOL IWinAmpActiveX
          class (AmpX.dll) version 2.4.0.6 installed via AOL Radio website.
          By setting an overly long value to 'ConvertFile()', an attacker can overrun
          a buffer and execute arbitrary code.
targets: Windows XP SP0-SP3 / Windows Vista SP0-SP1 / IE 6.0 SP0-2 & IE 7.0
exploits/windows/browser/aol_ampx_conver.reason: Requires victim to browse to a malicious page or site hosting the ActiveX exploit, violating the no-user-interaction rule.

exploits/windows/browser/aol_icq_downloa.name: America Online ICQ ActiveX Control Arbitrary File Download and Execute
exploits/windows/browser/aol_icq_downloa.description:
This module allows remote attackers to download and execute arbitrary files
          on a users system via the DownloadAgent function of the ICQPhone.SipxPhoneManager ActiveX control.
exploits/windows/browser/aol_icq_downloa.reason: Requires victim to browse to a malicious page that invokes the ActiveX control, so it is client-side interaction.

exploits/windows/browser/apple_itunes_pl.name: Apple ITunes 4.7 Playlist Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/apple_itunes_pl.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Apple ITunes 4.7
          build 4.7.0.42. By creating a URL link to a malicious PLS
          file, a remote attacker could overflow a buffer and execute
          arbitrary code. When using this module, be sure to set the
          URIPATH with an extension of '.pls'.
exploits/windows/browser/apple_itunes_pl.reason: Requires victim to open malicious .pls URL in browser, so it is client-side.

exploits/windows/browser/apple_quicktime.name: Apple QuickTime 7.6.7 _Marshaled_pUnk Code Execution
exploits/windows/browser/apple_quicktime.description:
This module exploits a memory trust issue in Apple QuickTime
          7.6.7. When processing a specially-crafted HTML page, the QuickTime ActiveX
          control will treat a supplied parameter as a trusted pointer. It will
          then use it as a COM-type pUnknown and lead to arbitrary code execution.

          This exploit utilizes a combination of heap spraying and the
          QuickTimeAuthoring.qtx module to bypass DEP and ASLR. This module does not
          opt-in to ASLR. As such, this module should be reliable on all Windows
          versions.

          NOTE: The addresses may need to be adjusted for older versions of QuickTime.
targets: Apple QuickTime Player 7.6.6 and 7.6.7 on Windows XP SP3
exploits/windows/browser/apple_quicktime.reason: Victim must open malicious HTML page in browser, requiring user interaction.

exploits/windows/browser/apple_quicktime.name: Apple QuickTime 7.7.2 MIME Type Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/apple_quicktime.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in Apple QuickTime 7.7.2. The stack
          based overflow occurs when processing a malformed Content-Type header. The module
          has been tested successfully on Safari 5.1.7 and 5.0.7 on Windows XP SP3.
exploits/windows/browser/apple_quicktime.reason: Requires victim to visit a malicious page with Safari/QuickTime, so it is client-side.

exploits/windows/browser/apple_quicktime.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in Apple Quicktime. The flaw is
          triggered when Quicktime fails to properly handle the data length for certain
          atoms such as 'rdrf' or 'dref' in the Alis record, which may result a buffer
          overflow by loading a specially crafted .mov file, and allows arbitrary
          code execution under the context of the current user.
targets: Quicktime 7.7.3 with IE 8 on Windows XP SP3
exploits/windows/browser/apple_quicktime.reason: Requires victim to open a malicious .mov file in browser, violating no-interaction rule.

exploits/windows/browser/apple_quicktime.name: Apple QuickTime 7.1.3 RTSP URI Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/apple_quicktime.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in Apple QuickTime
          7.1.3. This module was inspired by MOAB-01-01-2007.  The
          Browser target for this module was tested against IE 6 and
          Firefox 1.5.0.3 on Windows XP SP0/2; Firefox 3 blacklists the
          QuickTime plugin.
exploits/windows/browser/apple_quicktime.reason: Module requires victim to visit a malicious URL in browser

exploits/windows/browser/apple_quicktime.name: Apple QuickTime 7.6.6 Invalid SMIL URI Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/apple_quicktime.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in Apple QuickTime
          7.6.6. When processing a malformed SMIL uri, a stack-based buffer
          overflow can occur when logging an error message.
exploits/windows/browser/apple_quicktime.reason: Requires victim to open malicious SMIL file or visit page in browser (client-side interaction).

exploits/windows/browser/apple_quicktime.name: Apple QuickTime 7.7.2 TeXML Style Element font-table Field Stack Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/apple_quicktime.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in Apple QuickTime. When handling
          a TeXML file, it is possible to trigger a stack-based buffer overflow, and then
          gain arbitrary code execution under the context of the user.  This is due to the
          QuickTime3GPP.gtx component not handling certain Style subfields properly, as the
          font-table field, which is used to trigger the overflow in this module. Because of
          QuickTime restrictions when handling font-table fields, only 0x31-0x39 bytes can be
          used to overflow, so at the moment DEP/ASLR bypass hasn't been provided. The module
          has been tested successfully on IE6 and IE7 browsers (Windows XP and Vista).
exploits/windows/browser/apple_quicktime.reason: Module requires victim to visit a malicious URL in browser

exploits/windows/browser/ask_shortformat.name: Ask.com Toolbar askBar.dll ActiveX Control Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/ask_shortformat.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Ask.com Toolbar 4.0.2.53.
          An attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code by sending an overly
          long string to the "ShortFormat()" method in askbar.dll.
exploits/windows/browser/ask_shortformat.reason: Requires victim to visit a malicious web page (client-side interaction).

exploits/windows/browser/asus_net4switch.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in ASUS Net4Switch's ipswcom.dll
          ActiveX control.  A buffer overflow condition is possible in multiple places due
          to the use of the CxDbgPrint() function, which allows remote attackers to gain
          arbitrary code execution under the context of the user.
exploits/windows/browser/asus_net4switch.reason: Requires victim to open malicious web page in browser (client-side interaction).

exploits/windows/browser/athocgov_comple.name: AtHocGov IWSAlerts ActiveX Control Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/athocgov_comple.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in AtHocGov IWSAlerts. When
          sending an overly long string to the CompleteInstallation() method of AtHocGovTBr.dll
          (6.1.4.36) an attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code. This
          vulnerability was silently patched by the vendor.
targets: Windows XP SP0-SP3 / Windows Vista / IE 6.0 SP0-SP2 / IE 7
exploits/windows/browser/athocgov_comple.reason: Requires victim to browse to attacker page, so it is client-side not initial foothold.

exploits/windows/browser/autodesk_idrop.name: Autodesk IDrop ActiveX Control Heap Memory Corruption
exploits/windows/browser/autodesk_idrop.description:
This module exploits a heap-based memory corruption vulnerability in
          Autodesk IDrop ActiveX control (IDrop.ocx) version 17.1.51.160.
          An attacker can execute arbitrary code by triggering a heap use after
          free condition using the Src, Background, PackageXml properties.
targets: Windows XP SP0-SP3 / Windows Vista SP0-SP1 / IE 6.0 SP0-2 & IE 7.0
exploits/windows/browser/autodesk_idrop.reason: Requires victim to visit a malicious web page with IE, so it is client-side.

exploits/windows/browser/aventail_epi_ac.name: SonicWALL Aventail epi.dll AuthCredential Format String
exploits/windows/browser/aventail_epi_ac.description:
This module exploits a format string vulnerability within version 10.0.4.x and
          10.5.1 of the SonicWALL Aventail SSL-VPN Endpoint Interrogator/Installer ActiveX
          control (epi.dll). By calling the 'AuthCredential' method with a specially
          crafted Unicode format string, an attacker can cause memory corruption and
          execute arbitrary code.

          Unfortunately, it does not appear to be possible to indirectly re-use existing
          stack data for more reliable exploitation. This is due to several particulars
          about this vulnerability. First, the format string must be a Unicode string,
          which uses two bytes per character. Second, the buffer is allocated on the
          stack using the 'alloca' function. As such, each additional format specifier (%x)
          will add four more bytes to the size allocated. This results in the inability to
          move the read pointer outside of the buffer.

          Further testing showed that using specifiers that pop more than four bytes does
          not help. Any number of format specifiers will result in accessing the same value
          within the buffer.

          NOTE: It may be possible to leverage the vulnerability to leak memory contents.
          However, that has not been fully investigated at this time.
targets: epi.dll v10.0.4.18 on Windows XP SP3
exploits/windows/browser/aventail_epi_ac.reason: ActiveX browser exploit requires victim to visit malicious page with IE

exploits/windows/browser/awingsoft_web3d.name: AwingSoft Winds3D Player SceneURL Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/awingsoft_web3d.description:
This module exploits a data segment buffer overflow within Winds3D Viewer of
          AwingSoft Awakening 3.x (WindsPly.ocx v3.6.0.0). This ActiveX is a plugin of
          AwingSoft Web3D Player.
          By setting an overly long value to the 'SceneURL' property, an attacker can
          overrun a buffer and execute arbitrary code.
targets: Windows XP SP0-SP3 / IE 6.0 SP0-2 & IE 7.0
exploits/windows/browser/awingsoft_web3d.reason: Requires victim to visit a malicious web page with IE and the Winds3D ActiveX installed, so it is client-side interaction.

exploits/windows/browser/awingsoft_winds.name: AwingSoft Winds3D Player 3.5 SceneURL Download and Execute
exploits/windows/browser/awingsoft_winds.description:
This module exploits an untrusted program execution vulnerability within the
          Winds3D Player from AwingSoft. The Winds3D Player is a browser plugin for
          IE (ActiveX), Opera (DLL) and Firefox (XPI). By setting the 'SceneURL'
          parameter to the URL to an executable, an attacker can execute arbitrary
          code.

          Testing was conducted using plugin version 3.5.0.9 for Firefox 3.5 and
          IE 8 on Windows XP SP3.
exploits/windows/browser/awingsoft_winds.reason: Module requires victim to visit a malicious URL in browser

exploits/windows/browser/baofeng_storm_o.name: BaoFeng Storm mps.dll ActiveX OnBeforeVideoDownload Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/baofeng_storm_o.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in BaoFeng's Storm media Player ActiveX
          control. Versions of mps.dll including 3.9.4.27 and lower are affected. When passing
          an overly long string to the method "OnBeforeVideoDownload" an attacker can execute
          arbitrary code.
targets: Windows XP SP0-SP3 / Windows Vista / IE 6.0 SP0-SP2 / IE 7
exploits/windows/browser/baofeng_storm_o.reason: Requires victim to open malicious web page in IE with BaoFeng Storm ActiveX installed.

exploits/windows/browser/barcode_ax49.name: RKD Software BarCodeAx.dll v4.9 ActiveX Remote Stack Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/barcode_ax49.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in RKD Software Barcode Application
          ActiveX Control 'BarCodeAx.dll'. By sending an overly long string to the BeginPrint
          method of BarCodeAx.dll v4.9, an attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/browser/barcode_ax49.reason: Requires victim to open malicious web page or document that loads the vulnerable ActiveX control.

exploits/windows/browser/blackice_downlo.name: Black Ice Cover Page ActiveX Control Arbitrary File Download
exploits/windows/browser/blackice_downlo.description:
This module allows remote attackers to place arbitrary files on a users file system
          by abusing the "DownloadImageFileURL" method in the Black Ice BIImgFrm.ocx ActiveX
          Control (BIImgFrm.ocx 12.0.0.0).  Code execution can be achieved by first uploading the
          payload to the remote machine, and then upload another mof file, which enables Windows
          Management Instrumentation service to execute the binary. Please note that this module
          currently only works for Windows before Vista.  Also, a similar issue is reported in
          BIDIB.ocx (10.9.3.0) within the Barcode SDK.
exploits/windows/browser/blackice_downlo.reason: Requires the victim to browse to a malicious page (client-side interaction) and relies on an obscure third-party ActiveX control not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/browser/c6_messenger_do.name: Icona SpA C6 Messenger DownloaderActiveX Control Arbitrary File Download and Execute
exploits/windows/browser/c6_messenger_do.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability in Icona SpA C6 Messenger 1.0.0.1. The
          vulnerability is in the DownloaderActiveX Control (DownloaderActiveX.ocx). The
          insecure control can be abused to download and execute arbitrary files in the context of
          the currently logged-on user.
exploits/windows/browser/c6_messenger_do.reason: Requires the victim to visit a malicious page or interact with the browser to trigger the ActiveX control.

exploits/windows/browser/ca_brightstor_a.name: CA BrightStor ARCserve Backup AddColumn() ActiveX Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/ca_brightstor_a.description:
The CA BrightStor ARCserve Backup ActiveX control (ListCtrl.ocx) is vulnerable to a stack-based
          buffer overflow. By passing an overly long argument to the AddColumn() method, a remote attacker
          could overflow a buffer and execute arbitrary code on the system.
exploits/windows/browser/ca_brightstor_a.reason: Requires victim to visit a malicious web page that instantiates the vulnerable ActiveX control

exploits/windows/browser/chilkat_crypt_w.name: Chilkat Crypt ActiveX WriteFile Unsafe Method
exploits/windows/browser/chilkat_crypt_w.description:
This module allows attackers to execute code via the 'WriteFile' unsafe method of
          Chilkat Software Inc's Crypt ActiveX control.

          This exploit is based on shinnai's exploit that uses an hcp:// protocol URI to
          execute our payload immediately. However, this method requires that the victim user
          be browsing with Administrator. Additionally, this method will not work on newer
          versions of Windows.

          NOTE: This vulnerability is still unpatched. The latest version of Chilkat Crypt at
          the time of this writing includes ChilkatCrypt2.DLL version 4.4.4.0.

exploits/windows/browser/chrome_fileread.name: Chrome 72.0.3626.119 FileReader UaF exploit for Windows 7 x86
exploits/windows/browser/chrome_fileread.description:
This exploit takes advantage of a use after free vulnerability in Google
          Chrome 72.0.3626.119 running on Windows 7 x86.
          The FileReader.readAsArrayBuffer function can return multiple references to the
          same ArrayBuffer object, which can be freed and overwritten with sprayed objects.
          The dangling ArrayBuffer reference can be used to access the sprayed objects,
          allowing arbitrary memory access from Javascript. This is used to write and
          execute shellcode in a WebAssembly object.
          The shellcode is executed within the Chrome sandbox, so you must explicitly
          disable the sandbox for the payload to be successful.
exploits/windows/browser/chrome_fileread.reason: Module requires victim to visit a malicious URL in browser

exploits/windows/browser/cisco_anyconnec.name: Cisco AnyConnect VPN Client ActiveX URL Property Download and Execute
exploits/windows/browser/cisco_anyconnec.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability in the Cisco AnyConnect VPN client
          vpnweb.ocx ActiveX control. This control is typically used to install the
          VPN client. An attacker can set the 'url' property which is where the control
          tries to locate the files needed to install the client.

          The control tries to download two files from the site specified within the
          'url' property. One of these files it will be stored in a temporary directory and
          executed.
exploits/windows/browser/cisco_anyconnec.reason: Requires victim to visit attacker-controlled page in browser to trigger ActiveX download

exploits/windows/browser/cisco_playerpt_.name: Cisco Linksys PlayerPT ActiveX Control Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/cisco_playerpt_.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in Cisco Linksys PlayerPT 1.0.0.15
          as the installed with the web interface of Cisco Linksys WVC200 Wireless-G PTZ
          Internet Video Camera. The vulnerability, due to the insecure usage of sprintf in
          the SetSource method, allows to trigger a stack based buffer overflow which leads
          to code execution under the context of the user visiting a malicious web page.
exploits/windows/browser/cisco_playerpt_.reason: Requires victim to visit malicious web page (client-side interaction).

exploits/windows/browser/cisco_playerpt_.name: Cisco Linksys PlayerPT ActiveX Control SetSource sURL Argument Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/cisco_playerpt_.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in Cisco Linksys PlayerPT 1.0.0.15
          as the installed with the web interface of Cisco Linksys WVC200 Wireless-G PTZ
          Internet Video Camera. The vulnerability, due to the insecure usage of sprintf in
          the SetSource method, when handling a specially crafted sURL argument, allows to
          trigger a stack based buffer overflow which leads to code execution under the
          context of the user visiting a malicious web page.
exploits/windows/browser/cisco_playerpt_.reason: Requires the victim to browse to a malicious page, making it client-side.

exploits/windows/browser/cisco_webex_ext.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability present in the Cisco WebEx Chrome Extension
          version 1.0.1 which allows an attacker to execute arbitrary commands on a system.
exploits/windows/browser/cisco_webex_ext.reason: Module requires victim to visit a malicious URL in browser

exploits/windows/browser/citrix_gateway_.name: Citrix Gateway ActiveX Control Stack Based Buffer Overflow Vulnerability
exploits/windows/browser/citrix_gateway_.description:
This module exploits a stack based buffer overflow in the Citrix Gateway
          ActiveX control. Exploitation of this vulnerability requires user interaction.
          The victim must click a button in a dialog to begin a scan. This is typical
          interaction that users should be accustom to.

          Exploitation results in code execution with the privileges of the user who
          browsed to the exploit page.

exploits/windows/browser/clear_quest_cqo.description:
This module exploits a function prototype mismatch on the CQOle ActiveX
          control in IBM Rational ClearQuest < 7.1.1.9, < 7.1.2.6 or < 8.0.0.2 which
          allows reliable remote code execution when DEP isn't enabled.
exploits/windows/browser/clear_quest_cqo.reason: Requires victim to visit a malicious web page in IE, so it is client-side.

exploits/windows/browser/communicrypt_ma.name: CommuniCrypt Mail 1.16 SMTP ActiveX Stack Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/communicrypt_ma.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the ANSMTP.dll/AOSMTP.dll
          ActiveX Control provided by CommuniCrypt Mail 1.16.  By sending an overly
          long string to the "AddAttachments()" method, an attacker may be able to
          execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/browser/communicrypt_ma.reason: Requires victim to open malicious web page in IE, so it is client-side interaction.

exploits/windows/browser/creative_softwa.name: Creative Software AutoUpdate Engine ActiveX Control Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/creative_softwa.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Creative Software AutoUpdate Engine. When
          sending an overly long string to the cachefolder() property of CTSUEng.ocx
          an attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code.
targets: Windows XP SP0-SP3 / Windows Vista / IE 6.0 SP0-SP2 / IE 7
exploits/windows/browser/creative_softwa.reason: Requires victim to visit a malicious page in IE, so it is client-side interaction.

exploits/windows/browser/crystal_reports.description:
This module exploits a heap based buffer overflow in the CrystalPrintControl
          ActiveX, while handling the ServerResourceVersion property. The affected control
          can be found in the PrintControl.dll component as included with Crystal Reports
          2008. This module has been tested successfully on IE 6, 7 and 8 on Windows XP SP3
          and IE 8 on Windows 7 SP1. The module uses the msvcr71.dll library, loaded by the
          affected ActiveX control, to bypass DEP and ASLR.
exploits/windows/browser/crystal_reports.reason: Requires victim to visit a malicious page in IE to load the vulnerable ActiveX control.

exploits/windows/browser/dell_webcam_cra.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability in Dell Webcam's CrazyTalk component.
          Specifically, when supplying a long string for a file path to the BackImage
          property, an overflow may occur after checking certain file extension names,
          resulting in remote code execution under the context of the user.
exploits/windows/browser/dell_webcam_cra.reason: Requires victim to visit a malicious page or open a crafted file, so it is client-side.

exploits/windows/browser/dxstudio_player.name: Worldweaver DX Studio Player shell.execute() Command Execution
exploits/windows/browser/dxstudio_player.description:
This module exploits a command execution vulnerability within the DX
          Studio Player from Worldweaver for versions 3.0.29 and earlier. The
          player is a browser plugin for IE (ActiveX) and Firefox (dll). When an
          unsuspecting user visits a web page referring to a specially crafted
          .dxstudio document, an attacker can execute arbitrary commands.

          Testing was conducted using plugin version 3.0.29.0 for Firefox 2.0.0.20
          and IE 6 on Windows XP SP3. In IE, the user will be prompted if they
          wish to allow the plug-in to access local files. This prompt appears to
          occur only once per server host.

          NOTE: This exploit uses additionally dangerous script features to write
          to local files!

exploits/windows/browser/ea_checkrequire.name: Electronic Arts SnoopyCtrl ActiveX Control Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/ea_checkrequire.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Electronic Arts SnoopyCtrl
          ActiveX Control (NPSnpy.dll 1.1.0.36. When sending an overly long
          string to the CheckRequirements() method, an attacker may be able
          to execute arbitrary code.
targets: Windows XP SP0-SP3 / Windows Vista / IE 6.0 SP0-SP2 / IE 7
exploits/windows/browser/ea_checkrequire.reason: Requires victim to visit a malicious page in IE, making it client-side.

exploits/windows/browser/ebook_flipviewe.name: FlipViewer FViewerLoading ActiveX Control Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/ebook_flipviewe.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in E-BOOK Systems FlipViewer 4.0.
          The vulnerability is caused due to a boundary error in the
          FViewerLoading (FlipViewerX.dll) ActiveX control when handling the
          "LoadOpf()" method.
targets: Windows XP SP0-SP3 / Windows Vista / IE 6.0 SP0-SP2 / IE 7
exploits/windows/browser/ebook_flipviewe.reason: Requires victim to visit a malicious web page in IE, so it is client-side not initial remote foothold.

exploits/windows/browser/enjoysapgui_com.name: EnjoySAP SAP GUI ActiveX Control Arbitrary File Download
exploits/windows/browser/enjoysapgui_com.description:
This module allows remote attackers to place arbitrary files on a users file system
          by abusing the "Comp_Download" method in the SAP KWEdit ActiveX Control (kwedit.dll 6400.1.1.41).
exploits/windows/browser/enjoysapgui_com.reason: Requires the victim to browse to a malicious page that hosts the ActiveX control, so it needs user interaction.

exploits/windows/browser/enjoysapgui_pre.name: EnjoySAP SAP GUI ActiveX Control Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/enjoysapgui_pre.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in SAP KWEdit ActiveX
          Control (kwedit.dll 6400.1.1.41) provided by EnjoySAP GUI. By sending
          an overly long string to the "PrepareToPostHTML()" method, an attacker
          may be able to execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/browser/enjoysapgui_pre.reason: Requires victim to open malicious HTML in browser, so it is client-side not initial remote foothold.

exploits/windows/browser/example.name: Module Name
exploits/windows/browser/example.description:
This template covers IE8/9/10, and uses the user-agent HTTP header to detect
          the browser version.  Please note IE8 and newer may emulate an older IE version
          in compatibility mode, in that case the module won't be able to detect the
          browser correctly.
exploits/windows/browser/example.reason: Module requires victim to visit a malicious URL in browser

exploits/windows/browser/exodus.name: Exodus Wallet (ElectronJS Framework) remote Code Execution
exploits/windows/browser/exodus.description:
This module exploits a Remote Code Execution vulnerability in Exodus Wallet,
          a vulnerability in the ElectronJS Framework protocol handler can be used to
          get arbitrary command execution if the user clicks on a specially crafted URL.
exploits/windows/browser/exodus.reason: Requires the victim to click a crafted URL, making it a client-side attack.

exploits/windows/browser/facebook_extrac.name: Facebook Photo Uploader 4 ActiveX Control Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/facebook_extrac.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Facebook Photo Uploader 4.
          By sending an overly long string to the "ExtractIptc()" property located
          in the ImageUploader4.ocx (4.5.57.0) Control, an attacker may be able to execute
          arbitrary code.
targets: IE 6 SP0-SP2 / Windows XP SP2 Pro English
exploits/windows/browser/facebook_extrac.reason: Requires victim to visit a malicious web page in IE, so it is client-side interaction.

exploits/windows/browser/firefox_smil_ua.description:
This module exploits an out-of-bounds indexing/use-after-free condition present in
          nsSMILTimeContainer::NotifyTimeChange() across numerous versions of Mozilla Firefox
          on Microsoft Windows.
exploits/windows/browser/firefox_smil_ua.reason: Module requires victim to visit a malicious URL in browser

exploits/windows/browser/foxit_reader_pl.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability in the Foxit Reader Plugin, it exists in
          the npFoxitReaderPlugin.dll module. When loading PDF files from remote hosts,
          overly long query strings within URLs can cause a stack-based buffer overflow,
          which can be exploited to execute arbitrary code. This exploit has been tested
          on Windows 7 SP1 with Firefox 18.0 and Foxit Reader version 5.4.4.11281
          (npFoxitReaderPlugin.dll version 2.2.1.530).

exploits/windows/browser/getgodm_http_re.name: GetGo Download Manager HTTP Response Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/browser/getgodm_http_re.description:
