This module exploits the YoungZSoft CCProxy <= v6.2 suite
          Telnet service. The stack is overwritten when sending an overly
          long address to the 'ping' command.
exploits/windows/proxy/ccproxy_telnet_pi.reason: CCProxy is not installed by default on Windows VMs, making this third-party proxy software exploit non-replicable in a standard lab.

exploits/windows/proxy/proxypro_http_get.name: Proxy-Pro Professional GateKeeper 4.7 GET Request Overflow
exploits/windows/proxy/proxypro_http_get.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Proxy-Pro Professional
          GateKeeper 4.7. By sending a long HTTP GET to the default port
          of 3128, a remote attacker could overflow a buffer and execute
          arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/proxy/proxypro_http_get.reason: Targets third-party proxy software not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/proxy/qbik_wingate_wwwp.name: Qbik WinGate WWW Proxy Server URL Processing Overflow
exploits/windows/proxy/qbik_wingate_wwwp.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Qbik WinGate version
          6.1.1.1077 and earlier. By sending malformed HTTP POST URL to the
          HTTP proxy service on port 80, a remote attacker could overflow
          a buffer and execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/proxy/qbik_wingate_wwwp.reason: Targets a third-party proxy product not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/persistence/accessibili.name: Accessibility Features (Sticky Keys) Persistence via Debugger Registry Key
exploits/windows/persistence/accessibili.description:
This module makes it possible to apply the 'sticky keys' hack to a session with appropriate
          rights. The hack provides a means to get a SYSTEM shell using UI-level interaction at an RDP
          login screen or via a UAC confirmation dialog. The module modifies the Debug registry setting
          for certain executables.

          The module options allow for this hack to be applied to:

          SETHC   (sethc.exe is invoked when SHIFT is pressed 5 times),
          UTILMAN (Utilman.exe is invoked by pressing WINDOWS+U),
          OSK     (osk.exe is invoked by pressing WINDOWS+U, then launching the on-screen keyboard),
          DISP    (DisplaySwitch.exe is invoked by pressing WINDOWS+P),
          NARRATOR (Narrator.exe is invoked by pressing WINDOWS+CTR+ENTER),
          ATBROKER (AtBroker.exe is invoked by launching accessibility features from the login screen, such as WINDOWS+CTR+ENTER).

          Custom payloads and binaries can be run as part of this exploit, but must be manually uploaded
          to the target prior to running the module.
exploits/windows/persistence/accessibili.reason: Requires an existing session to modify registry for persistence, not initial remote foothold

exploits/windows/persistence/assistive_t.name: Assistive Technologies Persistence
exploits/windows/persistence/assistive_t.description:
This module achieves persistence by registering a custom Assistive Technology (AT) in the Windows registry.
          Then it configures the system to launch the AT executable during user logon or desktop switch (such as with
          an admin prived program).
          Requires Windows 8 or higher and administrative privileges.
exploits/windows/persistence/assistive_t.reason: Requires existing administrative session to install persistence, not initial remote foothold

exploits/windows/persistence/image_exec_.name: Windows Silent Process Exit Persistence
exploits/windows/persistence/image_exec_.description:
Windows allows you to set up a debug process when a process exits.
          This module uploads a payload and declares that it is the debug
          process to launch when a specified process exits.
exploits/windows/persistence/image_exec_.reason: Module is a persistence technique that requires an existing local session to set up, not initial remote foothold.

exploits/windows/persistence/linqpad_des.name: LINQPad Deserialization
exploits/windows/persistence/linqpad_des.description: This module exploits a bug in LIQPad up to version 5.48.00. The bug is only exploitable in paid version of software. The core of a bug is cache file containing deserialized data, which attacker can overwrite with malicious payload. The data gets deserialized every time the app restarts.
exploits/windows/persistence/linqpad_des.reason: Requires local access to overwrite a cache file and victim to restart the paid LINQPad app, not a network service exploit.

exploits/windows/persistence/notepadpp_p.name: Notepad++ Plugin Persistence
exploits/windows/persistence/notepadpp_p.description: This module create persistence by adding a malicious plugin to Notepad++, as it blindly loads and executes DLL from its plugin directory on startup, meaning that the payload will be executed every time Notepad++ is launched.
exploits/windows/persistence/notepadpp_p.reason: Requires local access to drop a malicious DLL into Notepad++ plugin folder, not a remote network exploit.

exploits/windows/persistence/registry.name: Windows Registry Only Persistence
exploits/windows/persistence/registry.description:
This module will install a payload that is executed during boot.
          It will be executed either at user logon or system startup via the registry
          value in "CurrentVersion\Run" or "RunOnce" (depending on privilege and selected method).
          The payload will be installed completely in registry.
exploits/windows/persistence/registry.reason: This is a persistence module that requires an existing session to install a registry payload, not an initial remote exploit.

exploits/windows/persistence/registry_ac.name: Windows Registry Active Setup Persistence
exploits/windows/persistence/registry_ac.description:
This module will register a payload to run via the Active Setup mechanism in Windows.
          Active Setup is a Windows feature that runs once per user at login.
          It triggers in a user context, losing privileges from admin to user.

          Active Setup will open a popup box with "Personalized Settings" and the text
          "Setting up personalized settings for: <SETUP_NAME>". However
          this won't occur until the login screen has exited (but before the desktop
          is loaded), and our execution is extremely fast so likely the user will not
          see it.
exploits/windows/persistence/registry_ac.reason: Requires an existing session to install persistence, not initial remote foothold.

exploits/windows/persistence/registry_us.name: Windows Registry Persistence via Userinit
exploits/windows/persistence/registry_us.description:
This module will install a payload that is executed during user logon.
          It writes a payload executable to disk and modifies the Userinit registry value
          in "HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon" to append the
          payload path, causing it to execute when any user logs in.
exploits/windows/persistence/registry_us.reason: Requires an existing session to write registry and drop payload, so it is privilege escalation, not initial foothold.

exploits/windows/persistence/service.name: Windows Persistent Service Installer
exploits/windows/persistence/service.description:
This Module will generate and upload an executable to a remote host.
          It will create a new service which will start the payload whenever the service is running. Admin or system
          privilege is required.
exploits/windows/persistence/service.reason: Module requires an existing admin-level session to install a persistent service, not initial remote foothold.

exploits/windows/persistence/startup_fol.name: Windows Persistent Startup Folder
exploits/windows/persistence/startup_fol.description:
This module establishes persistence by creating a payload in the user or system startup folder.
          Works on Vista and newer systems.
exploits/windows/persistence/startup_fol.reason: Requires an existing local session to plant the payload, so it is privilege-escalation/persistence, not initial remote foothold.

exploits/windows/persistence/task_schedu.name: Windows Persistent Task Scheduler
exploits/windows/persistence/task_schedu.description: This module establishes persistence by creating a scheduled task to run a payload.
exploits/windows/persistence/task_schedu.reason: Requires an existing session on the target to create a scheduled task for persistence.

exploits/windows/persistence/wsl/registr.name: Windows WSL via Registry Persistence
exploits/windows/persistence/wsl/registr.description:
This module will install a payload in WSL and execute it at user
          logon or system startup via the registry value in "CurrentVersion\Run"
          or "RunOnce" (depending on privilege and selected method).
          The payload will be installed completely in registry.

          Staged payloads, like fetch payloads in linux X64 don't tend to work. The payload
          will ask for the stage, then submit the HTTP fetch request
          and when the payload is sent it doesn't execute.

          `cmd/linux/http/x64/meterpreter_reverse_tcp` and unix cmd payloads tend to work.
exploits/windows/persistence/wsl/registr.reason: Module installs persistence in WSL via registry, requiring an existing session on the target.

exploits/windows/persistence/wmi/wmi_eve.name: WMI Event Subscription Event Log Persistence
exploits/windows/persistence/wmi/wmi_eve.description:
This module will create a permanent WMI event subscription to achieve file-less persistence using an event filter
          that will query the event log for an EVENT_ID_TRIGGER
          (default: failed logon request id 4625) that also contains a specified USERNAME_TRIGGER (note: failed logon auditing
          must be enabled on the target for this method to work, this can be enabled using "auditpol.exe /set /subcategory:Logon
          /failure:Enable"). When these criteria are met a command line event consumer will trigger an encoded powershell payload.

          Additionally a custom command can be specified to run once the trigger is
          activated using the advanced option CustomPsCommand. This module requires administrator level privileges as well as a
          high integrity process. It is also recommended to use staged payloads due to powershell script length limitations.
exploits/windows/persistence/wmi/wmi_eve.reason: Requires an existing administrator-level session to install persistence, not initial remote foothold.

exploits/windows/persistence/wmi/wmi_eve.name: WMI Event Subscription Interval Persistence
exploits/windows/persistence/wmi/wmi_eve.description:
This module will create a permanent WMI event subscription to achieve file-less persistence using an event filter
          that triggers the payload after the specified CALLBACK_INTERVAL.

          If the persistence is not installed, it will keep triggering payloads to spawn.

          Additionally a custom command can be specified to run once the trigger is
          activated using the advanced option CustomPsCommand. This module requires administrator level privileges as well as a
          high integrity process. It is also recommended to use staged payloads due to powershell script length limitations.
exploits/windows/persistence/wmi/wmi_eve.reason: Requires an existing high-integrity administrator session to install persistence, not initial remote foothold.

exploits/windows/persistence/wmi/wmi_eve.name: WMI Event Subscription Logon Timer Persistence
exploits/windows/persistence/wmi/wmi_eve.description:
This module will create a permanent WMI event subscription to achieve file-less persistence using an event filter that
          will trigger the payload after the system has a certain uptime. Payloads will trigger every minute until the set end time.

          Additionally a custom command can be specified to run once the trigger is
          activated using the advanced option CustomPsCommand. This module requires administrator level privileges as well as a
          high integrity process. It is also recommended to use staged payloads due to powershell script length limitations.
exploits/windows/persistence/wmi/wmi_eve.reason: Requires an existing administrator-level session, so it is post-exploitation persistence, not initial remote foothold.

exploits/windows/novell/file_reporter_fs.name: NFR Agent FSFUI Record File Upload RCE
exploits/windows/novell/file_reporter_fs.description:
NFRAgent.exe, a component of Novell File Reporter (NFR), allows remote attackers to upload
          arbitrary files via a directory traversal while handling requests to /FSF/CMD with
          FSFUI records with UICMD 130. This module has been tested successfully against NFR
          Agent 1.0.4.3 (File Reporter 1.0.2) and NFR Agent 1.0.3.22 (File Reporter 1.0.1).
exploits/windows/novell/file_reporter_fs.reason: Targets third-party Novell File Reporter agent service not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/novell/groupwisemesseng.name: Novell GroupWise Messenger Client Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/novell/groupwisemesseng.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Novell's GroupWise Messenger Client.
          By sending a specially crafted HTTP response, an attacker may be able to execute
          arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/novell/groupwisemesseng.reason: Targets a client application, not a network service, so requires victim interaction to trigger.

exploits/windows/novell/netiq_pum_eval.name: NetIQ Privileged User Manager 2.3.1 ldapagnt_eval() Remote Perl Code Execution
exploits/windows/novell/netiq_pum_eval.description:
This module abuses a lack of authorization in the NetIQ Privileged User Manager
          service (unifid.exe) to execute arbitrary perl code. The problem exists in the
          ldapagnt module. The module has been tested successfully on NetIQ PUM 2.3.1 over
          Windows 2003 SP2, which allows to execute arbitrary code with SYSTEM privileges.
targets: Windows 2003 SP2 / NetIQ Privileged User Manager 2.3.1
exploits/windows/novell/netiq_pum_eval.reason: Targets third-party NetIQ PUM service not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/novell/nmap_stor.name: Novell NetMail NMAP STOR Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/novell/nmap_stor.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Novell's Netmail 3.52 NMAP STOR
          verb. By sending an overly long string, an attacker can overwrite the
          buffer and control program execution.
exploits/windows/novell/nmap_stor.reason: Targets Novell NetMail, not a standard Windows service, and NetMail is rarely installed on lab VMs.

exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_desktop.name: Novell ZENworks 6.5 Desktop/Server Management Overflow
exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_desktop.description:
This module exploits a heap overflow in the Novell ZENworks
          Desktop Management agent. This vulnerability was discovered
          by Alex Wheeler.
exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_desktop.reason: Targets third-party Novell ZENworks agent not present on standard Windows VMs

exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_preboot.name: Novell ZENworks Configuration Management Preboot Service 0x21 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_preboot.description:
This module exploits a remote buffer overflow in the ZENworks Configuration
          Management 10 SP2. The vulnerability exists in the Preboot service and can be
          triggered by sending a specially crafted packet with the opcode 0x21
          (PROXY_CMD_FTP_FILE) to port 998/TCP. The module has been successfully tested on
          Novell ZENworks Configuration Management 10 SP2 and Windows Server 2003 SP2
          (DEP bypass).
targets: Novell ZENworks Configuration Management 10 SP2 / Windows 2003 SP2
exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_preboot.reason: Targets Novell ZENworks Preboot service, not a standard Windows service in typical lab VMs.

exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_preboot.name: Novell ZENworks Configuration Management Preboot Service 0x4c Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_preboot.description:
This module exploits a remote buffer overflow in the ZENworks Configuration
          Management. The vulnerability exists in the Preboot service and can be triggered
          by sending a specially crafted packet with the opcode 0x4c
          (PROXY_CMD_PREBOOT_TASK_INFO2) to port 998/TCP. The module has been successfully
          tested on Novell ZENworks Configuration Management 10 SP2 / SP3 and Windows Server
          2003 SP2 (DEP bypass).
targets: Novell ZENworks Configuration Management 10 SP3 / Windows 2003 SP2
exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_preboot.reason: Targets Novell ZENworks Preboot service, not a standard Windows service in a typical lab VM.

exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_preboot.name: Novell ZENworks Configuration Management Preboot Service 0x06 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_preboot.description:
This module exploits a remote buffer overflow in the ZENworks Configuration
          Management 10 SP2. The vulnerability exists in the Preboot service and can be
          triggered by sending a specially crafted packet with the opcode 0x06
          (PROXY_CMD_CLEAR_WS) to the 998/TCP port. The module has been successfully tested
          on Novell ZENworks Configuration Management 10 SP2 and Windows Server 2003 SP2
          (DEP bypass).
targets: Novell ZENworks Configuration Management 10 SP2 / Windows 2003 SP2
exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_preboot.reason: Targets third-party Novell ZENworks service not present on standard Windows VMs

exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_preboot.name: Novell ZENworks Configuration Management Preboot Service 0x6c Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_preboot.description:
This module exploits a remote buffer overflow in the ZENworks Configuration
          Management. The vulnerability exists in the Preboot service and can be triggered by
          sending a specially crafted packet with the opcode 0x6c (PROXY_CMD_GET_NEXT_STEP)
          to port 998/TCP. The module has been successfully tested on Novell ZENworks
          Configuration Management 10 SP2 / SP3 and Windows Server 2003 SP2 (DEP bypass).
targets: Novell ZENworks Configuration Management 10 SP3 / Windows 2003 SP2
exploits/windows/novell/zenworks_preboot.reason: Targets Novell ZENworks Preboot service, not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/nntp/ms05_030_nntp.name: MS05-030 Microsoft Outlook Express NNTP Response Parsing Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/nntp/ms05_030_nntp.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the news reader of Microsoft
          Outlook Express.
exploits/windows/nntp/ms05_030_nntp.reason: Requires victim to open a malicious NNTP server in Outlook Express (client-side interaction).

exploits/windows/nimsoft/nimcontroller_b.name: CA Unified Infrastructure Management Nimsoft 7.80 - Remote Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/nimsoft/nimcontroller_b.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow within the CA Unified Infrastructure Management nimcontroller.
          The vulnerability occurs in the robot (controller) component when sending a specially crafted directory_list
          probe.

          Technically speaking the target host must also be vulnerable to CVE-2020-8010 in order to reach the
          directory_list probe.
exploits/windows/nimsoft/nimcontroller_b.reason: Targets the Nimsoft monitoring controller service, which is not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/nfs/xlink_nfsd.name: Omni-NFS Server Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/nfs/xlink_nfsd.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Xlink Omni-NFS Server 5.2
          When sending a specially crafted nfs packet, an attacker may be able
          to execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/nfs/xlink_nfsd.reason: Omni-NFS is third-party software not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/motorola/timbuktu_fileu.name: Timbuktu Pro Directory Traversal/File Upload
exploits/windows/motorola/timbuktu_fileu.description:
This module exploits a directory traversal vulnerability in Motorola's
          Timbuktu Pro for Windows 8.6.5.
exploits/windows/motorola/timbuktu_fileu.reason: Targets the Timbuktu Pro application, which is not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/mmsp/ms10_025_wmss_conn.name: Windows Media Services ConnectFunnel Stack Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/mmsp/ms10_025_wmss_conn.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the Windows Media
          Unicast Service version 4.1.0.3930 (NUMS.exe). By sending a specially
          crafted FunnelConnect request, an attacker can execute arbitrary code
          under the "NetShowServices" user account. Windows Media Services 4.1 ships
          with Windows 2000 Server, but is not installed by default.

          NOTE: This service does NOT restart automatically. Successful, as well as
          unsuccessful exploitation attempts will kill the service which prevents
          additional attempts.
exploits/windows/mmsp/ms10_025_wmss_conn.reason: Windows Media Services is not installed by default on Windows 2000 Server/Pro, so the vulnerable service is absent in a standard VM.

exploits/windows/misc/achat_bof.name: Achat Unicode SEH Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/achat_bof.description:
This module exploits a Unicode SEH buffer overflow in Achat. By
          sending a crafted message to the default port 9256/UDP, it's possible to overwrite the
          SEH handler. Even when the exploit is reliable, it depends on timing since there are
          two threads overflowing the stack in the same time. This module has been tested on
          Achat v0.150 running on Windows XP SP3 and Windows 7.
targets: Achat beta v0.150 / Windows XP SP3 / Windows 7 SP1
exploits/windows/misc/achat_bof.reason: Targets third-party chat software Achat not present on standard Windows VMs

exploits/windows/misc/actfax_raw_server_.name: ActFax 5.01 RAW Server Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/actfax_raw_server_.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability in ActFax Server 5.01 RAW server. The RAW
          Server can be used to transfer fax messages without any underlying protocols. To
          note significant fields in the fax being transferred, like the fax number or the
          recipient, ActFax data fields can be used. This module exploits a buffer overflow
          in the handling of the @F506 fields due to the insecure usage of strcpy. This
          module has been tested successfully on ActFax 5.01 over Windows XP SP3 (English).
exploits/windows/misc/actfax_raw_server_.reason: Targets third-party fax server software not present on standard Windows VMs

exploits/windows/misc/agentxpp_receive_a.name: AgentX++ Master AgentX::receive_agentx Stack Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/agentxpp_receive_a.description:
This exploits a stack buffer overflow in the AgentX++ library, as used by
          various applications. By sending a specially crafted request, an attacker can
          execute arbitrary code, potentially with SYSTEM privileges.

          This module was tested successfully against master.exe as included with Real
          Network\'s Helix Server v12. When installed as a service with Helix Server,
          the service runs as SYSTEM, has no recovery action, but will start automatically
          on boot.

          This module does not work with NX/XD enabled but could be modified easily to
          do so. The address
targets: Helix Server v12 and v13 - master.exe
exploits/windows/misc/agentxpp_receive_a.reason: Targets Helix Server, a non-standard third-party streaming server not present on default Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/allmediaserver_bof.name: ALLMediaServer 0.8 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/allmediaserver_bof.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in ALLMediaServer 0.8. The vulnerability
          is caused due to a boundary error within the handling of HTTP request.

          While the exploit supports DEP bypass via ROP, on Windows 7 the stack pivoting isn't
          reliable across virtual (VMWare, VirtualBox) and physical environments. Because of
          this the module isn't using DEP bypass on the Windows 7 SP1 target, where by default
          DEP is OptIn and AllMediaServer won't run with DEP.
exploits/windows/misc/allmediaserver_bof.reason: Targets obscure third-party media server not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/altiris_ds_sqli.name: Symantec Altiris DS SQL Injection
exploits/windows/misc/altiris_ds_sqli.description:
This module exploits a SQL injection flaw in Symantec Altiris Deployment Solution 6.8
          to 6.9.164. The vulnerability exists on axengine.exe which fails to adequately sanitize
          numeric input fields in "UpdateComputer" notification Requests. In order to spawn a shell,
          several SQL injections are required in close succession, first to enable xp_cmdshell, then
          retrieve the payload via TFTP and finally execute it. The module also has the capability
          to disable or enable local application authentication. In order to work the target system
          must have a tftp client available.
exploits/windows/misc/altiris_ds_sqli.reason: Targets a third-party management product not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/apple_quicktime_rt.name: Apple QuickTime 7.3 RTSP Response Header Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/apple_quicktime_rt.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Apple QuickTime 7.3. By sending an overly long
          RTSP response to a client, an attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/misc/apple_quicktime_rt.reason: Requires the victim to connect to an attacker RTSP server (client-side interaction), so it cannot give an initial foothold without user action.

exploits/windows/misc/asus_dpcproxy_over.name: Asus Dpcproxy Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/asus_dpcproxy_over.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Asus Dpcroxy version 2.0.0.19.
          It should be vulnerable until version 2.0.0.24.
          Credit to Luigi Auriemma
exploits/windows/misc/asus_dpcproxy_over.reason: Targets third-party Asus software not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/avaya_winpmd_uniho.name: Avaya WinPMD UniteHostRouter Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/avaya_winpmd_uniho.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Avaya WinPMD. The vulnerability
          exists in the UniteHostRouter service, due to the insecure usage of memcpy when
          parsing specially crafted "To:" headers. The module has been tested successfully on
          Avaya WinPMD 3.8.2 over Windows XP SP3 and Windows 2003 SP2.
exploits/windows/misc/avaya_winpmd_uniho.reason: Targets obscure third-party Avaya WinPMD software not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/avidphoneticindexe.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in process
          AvidPhoneticIndexer.exe (port 4659), which comes as part of the Avid Media Composer
          5.5 Editing Suite. This daemon sometimes starts on a different port; if you start
          it standalone it will run on port 4660.
exploits/windows/misc/avidphoneticindexe.reason: Targets obscure third-party software not present on standard Windows VMs

exploits/windows/misc/bakbone_netvault_h.name: BakBone NetVault Remote Heap Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/bakbone_netvault_h.description:
This module exploits a heap overflow in the BakBone NetVault
          Process Manager service. This code is a direct port of the netvault.c
          code written by nolimit and BuzzDee.
exploits/windows/misc/bakbone_netvault_h.reason: Targets a non-standard third-party backup service not present on typical Windows lab VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/bcaaa_bof.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in process bcaaa-130.exe (port 16102),
          which comes as part of the Blue Coat Authentication proxy.  Please note that by default,
          this exploit will attempt up to three times in order to successfully gain remote code
          execution (in some cases, it takes as many as five times).  This can cause your activity
          to look even more suspicious.  To modify the number of exploit attempts, set the
          ATTEMPTS option.
exploits/windows/misc/bcaaa_bof.reason: Targets Blue Coat Authentication proxy bcaaa-130.exe, not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/bigant_server.name: BigAnt Server 2.2 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/bigant_server.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in BigAnt Server 2.2.
          By sending a specially crafted packet, an attacker may be
          able to execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/misc/bigant_server.reason: Targets BigAnt Server, a third-party IM server not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/bigant_server_250.name: BigAnt Server 2.50 SP1 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/bigant_server_250.description:
This exploits a stack buffer overflow in the BigAnt Messaging Service,
          part of the BigAnt Server product suite. This module was tested
          successfully against version 2.50 SP1.
exploits/windows/misc/bigant_server_250.reason: BigAnt is third-party messaging software not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/bigant_server_dupf.name: BigAnt Server DUPF Command Arbitrary File Upload
exploits/windows/misc/bigant_server_dupf.description:
This exploits an arbitrary file upload vulnerability in BigAnt Server 2.97 SP7.
          A lack of authentication allows to make unauthenticated file uploads through a DUPF
          command. Additionally the filename option in the same command can be used to launch
          a directory traversal attack and achieve arbitrary file upload.

          The module uses the Windows Management Instrumentation service to execute an
          arbitrary payload on vulnerable installations of BigAnt on Windows XP and 2003. It
          has been successfully tested on BigAnt Server 2.97 SP7 over Windows XP SP3 and 2003
          SP2.
exploits/windows/misc/bigant_server_dupf.reason: Targets BigAnt Server, a third-party chat application not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/bigant_server_sch_.name: BigAnt Server 2 SCH And DUPF Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/bigant_server_sch_.description:
This exploits a stack buffer overflow in BigAnt Server 2.97 SP7. The
          vulnerability is due to the dangerous usage of strcpy while handling errors. This
          module uses a combination of SCH and DUPF request to trigger the vulnerability, and
          has been tested successfully against version 2.97 SP7 over Windows XP SP3 and
          Windows 2003 SP2.
targets: BigAnt Server 2.97 SP7 / Windows XP SP3
exploits/windows/misc/bigant_server_sch_.reason: Targets BigAnt Server, a third-party IM server not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/bigant_server_usv.name: BigAnt Server 2.52 USV Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/bigant_server_usv.description:
This exploits a stack buffer overflow in the BigAnt Messaging Service,
          part of the BigAnt Server product suite. This module was tested
          successfully against version 2.52.

          NOTE: The AntServer service does not restart, you only get one shot.
exploits/windows/misc/bigant_server_usv.reason: Targets BigAnt messaging service which is not present on standard Windows VMs

exploits/windows/misc/bomberclone_overfl.name: Bomberclone 0.11.6 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/bomberclone_overfl.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Bomberclone 0.11.6 for Windows.
          The return address is overwritten with lstrcpyA memory address,
          the second and third value are the destination buffer,
          the fourth value is the source address of our buffer in the stack.
          This exploit is like a return in libc.

          ATTENTION
          The shellcode is exec ONLY when someone try to close bomberclone.
exploits/windows/misc/bomberclone_overfl.reason: Targets obscure third-party game Bomberclone, not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/bopup_comm.name: Bopup Communications Server Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/bopup_comm.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Bopup Communications Server 3.2.26.5460.
          By sending a specially crafted packet, an attacker may be
          able to execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/misc/bopup_comm.reason: Targets a third-party IM server not present on standard Windows VMs

exploits/windows/misc/borland_interbase.name: Borland Interbase Create-Request Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/borland_interbase.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Borland Interbase 2007.
          By sending a specially crafted create-request packet, a remote
          attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/misc/borland_interbase.reason: Targets Borland InterBase 2007, a database server not installed by default on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/borland_starteam.name: Borland CaliberRM StarTeam Multicast Service Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/borland_starteam.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Borland CaliberRM 2006. By sending
          a specially crafted GET request to the STMulticastService, an attacker may be
          able to execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/misc/borland_starteam.reason: Targets an obscure third-party service not present on standard Windows VMs

exploits/windows/misc/citrix_streamproce.name: Citrix Provisioning Services 5.6 streamprocess.exe Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/citrix_streamproce.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Citrix Provisioning Services 5.6.
          By sending a specially crafted packet to the Provisioning Services server, a fixed
          length buffer on the stack can be overflowed and arbitrary code can be executed.
targets: Windows XP SP3 / Windows Server 2003 SP2 / Windows Vista
exploits/windows/misc/citrix_streamproce.reason: Targets Citrix Provisioning Services, not present on standard Windows lab VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/citrix_streamproce.name: Citrix Provisioning Services 5.6 SP1 Streamprocess Opcode 0x40020000 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/citrix_streamproce.description:
This module exploits a remote buffer overflow in the Citrix Provisioning Services
          5.6 SP1 (without Hotfix CPVS56SP1E043) by sending a malformed packet to the
          6905/UDP port.  The module has been successfully tested on Windows Server 2003 SP2,
          Windows 7, and Windows XP SP3.
exploits/windows/misc/citrix_streamproce.reason: Exploits a network service on UDP/6905 and yields remote code execution without credentials or user interaction.

exploits/windows/misc/citrix_streamproce.name: Citrix Provisioning Services 5.6 SP1 Streamprocess Opcode 0x40020004 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/citrix_streamproce.description:
This module exploits a remote buffer overflow in the Citrix Provisioning Services
          5.6 SP1 (without Hotfix CPVS56SP1E043) by sending a malformed packet with the opcode
          0x40020004 (GetBootRecordRequest) to the 6905/UDP port. The module, which allows
          code execution under the context of SYSTEM, has been successfully tested on Windows Server
          2003 SP2 and Windows XP SP3.
exploits/windows/misc/citrix_streamproce.reason: Targets Citrix Provisioning Services, not present on standard Windows VMs

exploits/windows/misc/citrix_streamproce.name: Citrix Provisioning Services 5.6 SP1 Streamprocess Opcode 0x40020002 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/citrix_streamproce.description:
This module exploits a remote buffer overflow in the Citrix Provisioning Services
          5.6 SP1 (without Hotfix CPVS56SP1E043) by sending a malformed packet with the opcode
          0x40020002 (GetFooterRequest) to the 6905/UDP port. The module, which allows code execution
          under the context of SYSTEM, has been successfully tested on Windows Server 2003 SP2
          and Windows XP SP3.
exploits/windows/misc/citrix_streamproce.reason: Targets third-party Citrix Provisioning Services, not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/citrix_streamproce.name: Citrix Provisioning Services 5.6 SP1 Streamprocess Opcode 0x40020006 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/citrix_streamproce.description:
This module exploits a remote buffer overflow in the Citrix Provisioning Services
          5.6 SP1 (without Hotfix CPVS56SP1E043) by sending a malformed packet with the opcode
          0x40020006 (GetObjetsRequest) to the 6905/UDP port. The module, which allows code execution
          under the context of SYSTEM, has been successfully tested on Windows Server 2003 SP2
          and Windows XP SP3.
exploits/windows/misc/citrix_streamproce.reason: Exploits a network service on UDP/6905 without credentials or user interaction to gain SYSTEM shell.

exploits/windows/misc/cloudme_sync.name: CloudMe Sync v1.10.9
exploits/windows/misc/cloudme_sync.description:
This module exploits a stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability
          in CloudMe Sync v1.10.9 client application. This module has been
          tested successfully on Windows 7 SP1 x86.
exploits/windows/misc/cloudme_sync.reason: Targets a third-party desktop sync app not present on standard Windows VMs

exploits/windows/misc/commvault_cmd_exec.name: Commvault Communications Service (cvd) Command Injection
exploits/windows/misc/commvault_cmd_exec.description:
This module exploits a command injection vulnerability
          discovered in Commvault Service v11 SP5 and earlier versions (tested in v11 SP5
          and v10). The vulnerability exists in the cvd.exe service and allows an
          attacker to execute arbitrary commands in the context of the service. By
          default, the Commvault Communications service installs and runs as SYSTEM in
          Windows and does not require authentication. This vulnerability was discovered
          in the Windows version. The Linux version wasn't tested.
targets: Commvault Communications Service (cvd) / Microsoft Windows 7 and higher
exploits/windows/misc/commvault_cmd_exec.reason: Commvault is not installed by default on standard Windows VMs, so the service is absent.

exploits/windows/misc/crosschex_device_b.name: Anviz CrossChex Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/crosschex_device_b.description:
Waits for broadcasts from Ainz CrossChex looking for new devices, and returns a custom broadcast,
          triggering a stack buffer overflow.
exploits/windows/misc/crosschex_device_b.reason: Targets a third-party device-management application not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/cve_2022_28381_all.name: ALLMediaServer 1.6 SEH Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/cve_2022_28381_all.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow leading to a SEH handler overwrite
          in ALLMediaServer 1.6. The vulnerability is caused due to a boundary error
          within the handling of a HTTP request. Note that this exploit will only work
          against x86 or WoW64 targets, x64 is not supported at this time.
exploits/windows/misc/cve_2022_28381_all.reason: Targets obscure third-party media server not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/delta_electronics_.name: Delta Electronics InfraSuite Device Master Deserialization
exploits/windows/misc/delta_electronics_.description:
Delta Electronics InfraSuite Device Master versions below v1.0.5 have an
          unauthenticated .NET deserialization vulnerability within the 'ParseUDPPacket()'
          method of the 'Device-Gateway-Status' process.

          The 'ParseUDPPacket()' method reads user-controlled packet data and eventually
          calls 'BinaryFormatter.Deserialize()' on what it determines to be the packet header without appropriate validation,
          leading to unauthenticated code execution as the user running the 'Device-Gateway-Status' process.
exploits/windows/misc/delta_electronics_.reason: Targets specialized industrial software (Delta InfraSuite) not present on standard Windows lab VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/disk_savvy_adm.name: Disk Savvy Enterprise v10.4.18
exploits/windows/misc/disk_savvy_adm.description:
This module exploits a stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability
          in Disk Savvy Enterprise v10.4.18, caused by improper bounds
          checking of the request sent to the built-in server. This module
          has been tested successfully on Windows 7 SP1 x86.
exploits/windows/misc/disk_savvy_adm.reason: Disk Savvy Enterprise is not installed by default on Windows VMs, making it unavailable in a standard lab.

exploits/windows/misc/doubletake.name: DoubleTake/HP StorageWorks Storage Mirroring Service Authentication Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/doubletake.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the authentication mechanism of
          NSI Doubletake which is also rebranded as HP Storage Works. This vulnerability
          was found by Titon of Bastard Labs.
exploits/windows/misc/doubletake.reason: Exploits a network service (Storage Mirroring) listening on TCP 1100, giving remote code execution without credentials or user interaction.

exploits/windows/misc/eiqnetworks_esa.name: eIQNetworks ESA License Manager LICMGR_ADDLICENSE Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/eiqnetworks_esa.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in eIQnetworks
          Enterprise Security Analyzer. During the processing of
          long arguments to the LICMGR_ADDLICENSE command, a stack-based
          buffer overflow occurs. This module has only been tested
          against ESA v2.1.13.
exploits/windows/misc/eiqnetworks_esa.reason: Targets obscure third-party software not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/eiqnetworks_esa_to.name: eIQNetworks ESA Topology DELETEDEVICE Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/eiqnetworks_esa_to.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in eIQnetworks
          Enterprise Security Analyzer. During the processing of
          long arguments to the DELETEDEVICE command in the Topology
          server, a stack-based buffer overflow occurs.

          This module has only been tested against ESA v2.1.13.
exploits/windows/misc/eiqnetworks_esa_to.reason: Targets a third-party network-monitoring product not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/enterasys_netsight.name: Enterasys NetSight nssyslogd.exe Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/enterasys_netsight.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Enterasys NetSight. The
          vulnerability exists in the Syslog service (nssylogd.exe) when parsing a specially
          crafted PRIO from a syslog message. The module has been tested successfully on
          Enterasys NetSight 4.0.1.34 over Windows XP SP3 and Windows 2003 SP2.
exploits/windows/misc/enterasys_netsight.reason: Targets proprietary Enterasys NetSight software, not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/eureka_mail_err.name: Eureka Email 2.2q ERR Remote Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/eureka_mail_err.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in the Eureka Email 2.2q
          client that is triggered through an excessively long ERR message.

          NOTE: this exploit isn't very reliable. Unfortunately reaching the
          vulnerable code can only be done when manually checking mail (Ctrl-M).
          Checking at startup will not reach the code targeted here.
exploits/windows/misc/eureka_mail_err.reason: Requires the victim to manually check mail (Ctrl-M) inside the email client, so victim interaction is mandatory.

exploits/windows/misc/fb_cnct_group.name: Firebird Relational Database CNCT Group Number Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/fb_cnct_group.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability in Firebird SQL Server.  A specially
        crafted packet can be sent which will overwrite a pointer allowing the attacker to
        control where data is read from.  Shortly, following the controlled read, the
        pointer is called resulting in code execution.

        The vulnerability exists with a group number extracted from the CNCT information,
        which is sent by the client, and whose size is not properly checked.

        This module uses an existing call to memcpy, just prior to the vulnerable code,
        which allows a small amount of data to be written to the stack. A two-phases
        stack pivot allows to execute the ROP chain which ultimately is used to execute
        VirtualAlloc and bypass DEP.
exploits/windows/misc/fb_cnct_group.reason: Exploits a network service (Firebird SQL) listening on port 3050, gives remote code execution without credentials or user interaction.

exploits/windows/misc/fb_isc_attach_data.name: Firebird Relational Database isc_attach_database() Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/fb_isc_attach_data.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Borland InterBase
          by sending a specially crafted create request.
exploits/windows/misc/fb_isc_attach_data.reason: Targets Firebird database service not installed by default on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/fb_isc_create_data.name: Firebird Relational Database isc_create_database() Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/fb_isc_create_data.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Borland InterBase
          by sending a specially crafted create request.
exploits/windows/misc/fb_isc_create_data.reason: Targets Firebird database, not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/fb_svc_attach.name: Firebird Relational Database SVC_attach() Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/fb_svc_attach.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Borland InterBase
          by sending a specially crafted service attach request.
exploits/windows/misc/fb_svc_attach.reason: Targets Firebird/InterBase database service, not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/gh0st.name: Gh0st Client buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/gh0st.description: This module exploits a Memory buffer overflow in the Gh0st client (C2 server)
exploits/windows/misc/gh0st.reason: Exploits a C2 client, not a network service, so it cannot give initial remote foothold

exploits/windows/misc/gimp_script_fu.name: GIMP script-fu Server Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/gimp_script_fu.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in the script-fu server
          component on GIMP <= 2.6.12. By sending a specially crafted packet, an
          attacker may be able to achieve remote code execution under the context
          of the user.

          This module has been tested on GIMP for Windows from installers
          provided by Jernej Simoncic.
targets: GIMP 2.6.10 (no DEP) / Windows XP SP3 / Windows 7 SP1
exploits/windows/misc/gimp_script_fu.reason: GIMP is not installed by default on standard Windows VMs and requires third-party software.

exploits/windows/misc/hp_dataprotector_c.name: HP Data Protector 8.10 Remote Command Execution
exploits/windows/misc/hp_dataprotector_c.description:
This module exploits a remote command execution on HP Data Protector 8.10. Arbitrary
          commands can be executed by sending crafted requests with opcode 28 to the OmniInet
          service listening on the TCP/5555 port. Since there is a strict length limitation on
          the command, rundll32.exe is executed, and the payload is provided through a DLL by a
          fake SMB server. This module has been tested successfully on HP Data Protector 8.1 on
          Windows 7 SP1.
exploits/windows/misc/hp_dataprotector_c.reason: Targets third-party backup software not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/hp_dataprotector_d.name: HP Data Protector DtbClsLogin Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/hp_dataprotector_d.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in HP Data Protector 4.0 SP1. The
          overflow occurs during the login process, in the DtbClsLogin function provided by
          the dpwindtb.dll component, where the Utf8Cpy (strcpy like function) is used in an
          insecure way with the username. A successful exploitation will lead to code execution
          with the privileges of the "dpwinsdr.exe" (HP Data Protector Express Domain Server
          Service) process, which runs as SYSTEM by default.
targets: HP Data Protector Express 4.0 SP1 (build 43064) / Windows XP SP3
exploits/windows/misc/hp_dataprotector_d.reason: Targets HP Data Protector, not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/hp_dataprotector_e.name: HP Data Protector Backup Client Service Remote Code Execution
exploits/windows/misc/hp_dataprotector_e.description:
This module abuses the Backup Client Service (OmniInet.exe) to achieve remote code
          execution. The vulnerability exists in the EXEC_BAR operation, which allows to
          execute arbitrary processes. This module has been tested successfully on HP Data
          Protector 6.20 on Windows 2003 SP2 and Windows 2008 R2.
targets: HP Data Protector 6.20 build 370 / VBScript CMDStager
exploits/windows/misc/hp_dataprotector_e.reason: Targets HP Data Protector, not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/hp_dataprotector_i.name: HP Data Protector 6.10/6.11/6.20 Install Service
exploits/windows/misc/hp_dataprotector_i.description:
This module exploits HP Data Protector OmniInet process on Windows only.
          This exploit invokes the install service function which allows an attacker to create a
          custom payload in the format of an executable.

          To ensure this works, the SMB server created in MSF must have a share called Omniback
          which has a subfolder i386, i.e. \192.168.1.1\Omniback\i386\
exploits/windows/misc/hp_dataprotector_i.reason: Requires third-party HP Data Protector software not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/hp_dataprotector_t.name: HP Data Protector Backup Client Service Directory Traversal
exploits/windows/misc/hp_dataprotector_t.description:
This module exploits a directory traversal vulnerability in the Hewlett-Packard Data
          Protector product. The vulnerability exists in the Backup Client Service (OmniInet.exe)
          and is triggered when parsing packets with opcode 42. This module has been tested
          successfully on HP Data Protector 6.20 on Windows 2003 SP2 and Windows XP SP3.
targets: HP Data Protector 6.20 build 370 / Windows 2003 SP2
exploits/windows/misc/hp_dataprotector_t.reason: Targets a non-default third-party backup product not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/hp_imc_dbman_resta.name: HPE iMC dbman RestartDB Unauthenticated RCE
exploits/windows/misc/hp_imc_dbman_resta.description:
This module exploits a remote command execution vulnerablity in
          Hewlett Packard Enterprise Intelligent Management Center before
          version 7.3 E0504P04.

          The dbman service allows unauthenticated remote users to restart
          a user-specified database instance (OpCode 10008), however the
          instance ID is not sanitized, allowing execution of arbitrary
          operating system commands as SYSTEM. This service listens on
          TCP port 2810 by default.

          This module has been tested successfully on iMC PLAT v7.2 (E0403)
          on Windows 7 SP1 (EN).
exploits/windows/misc/hp_imc_dbman_resta.reason: Targets HPE iMC, not a default Windows service, so unlikely to be present in a standard Windows VM lab.

exploits/windows/misc/hp_imc_dbman_resto.name: HPE iMC dbman RestoreDBase Unauthenticated RCE
exploits/windows/misc/hp_imc_dbman_resto.description:
This module exploits a remote command execution vulnerablity in
          Hewlett Packard Enterprise Intelligent Management Center before
          version 7.3 E0504P04.

          The dbman service allows unauthenticated remote users to restore
          a user-specified database (OpCode 10007), however the database
          connection username is not sanitized resulting in command injection,
          allowing execution of arbitrary operating system commands as SYSTEM.
          This service listens on TCP port 2810 by default.

          This module has been tested successfully on iMC PLAT v7.2 (E0403)
          on Windows 7 SP1 (EN).
exploits/windows/misc/hp_imc_dbman_resto.reason: Targets HPE iMC management software, not present on standard Windows Server/Desktop VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/hp_imc_uam.name: HP Intelligent Management Center UAM Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/hp_imc_uam.description:
This module exploits a remote buffer overflow in HP Intelligent Management Center
          UAM. The vulnerability exists in the uam.exe component, when using sprint in a
          insecure way for logging purposes. The vulnerability can be triggered by sending a
          malformed packet to the 1811/UDP port. The module has been successfully tested on
          HP iMC 5.0 E0101 and UAM 5.0 E0102 over Windows Server 2003 SP2 (DEP bypass).
targets: HP iMC 5.0 E0101 / UAM 5.0 E0102 on Windows 2003 SP2
exploits/windows/misc/hp_imc_uam.reason: HP iMC is not installed by default on standard Windows VMs, making the service unavailable for remote exploitation.

exploits/windows/misc/hp_loadrunner_mage.name: HP LoadRunner magentproc.exe Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/hp_loadrunner_mage.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in HP LoadRunner before 11.52. The
          vulnerability exists on the LoadRunner Agent Process magentproc.exe. By sending
          a specially crafted packet, an attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/misc/hp_loadrunner_mage.reason: HP LoadRunner is not installed by default on standard Windows VMs, making this unsupported for typical lab setups.

exploits/windows/misc/hp_loadrunner_mage.description:
This module exploits a remote command execution vulnerablity in HP LoadRunner before 9.50
          and also HP Performance Center before 9.50. HP LoadRunner 12.53 and other versions are
          also most likely vulneable if the (non-default) SSL option is turned off.
          By sending a specially crafted packet, an attacker can execute commands remotely.
          The service is vulnerable provided the Secure Channel feature is disabled (default).
exploits/windows/misc/hp_loadrunner_mage.reason: HP LoadRunner is not installed by default on standard Windows VMs, making this unsupported for typical lab setups.

exploits/windows/misc/hp_magentservice.name: HP Diagnostics Server magentservice.exe Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/hp_magentservice.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in HP Diagnostics Server
          magentservice.exe service. By sending a specially crafted packet, an attacker
          may be able to execute arbitrary code. Originally found and posted by
          AbdulAziz Harir via ZDI.
exploits/windows/misc/hp_magentservice.reason: Targets a non-standard third-party HP Diagnostics Server service not present on default Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/hp_omniinet_1.name: HP OmniInet.exe MSG_PROTOCOL Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/hp_omniinet_1.description:
This module exploits a stack-based buffer overflow in the Hewlett-Packard
          OmniInet NT Service. By sending a specially crafted MSG_PROTOCOL (0x010b)
          packet, a remote attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code with elevated
          privileges.

          This service is installed with HP OpenView Data Protector, HP Application
          Recovery Manager and potentially other products. This exploit has been tested
          against versions 6.1, 6.0, and 5.50 of Data Protector. and versions 6.0 and 6.1
          of Application Recovery Manager.

          NOTE: There are actually two consecutive wcscpy() calls in the program (which
          may be why ZDI considered them two separate issues). However, this module only
          exploits the first one.
exploits/windows/misc/hp_omniinet_1.reason: Targets HP OmniInet service which is not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/hp_omniinet_2.name: HP OmniInet.exe MSG_PROTOCOL Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/hp_omniinet_2.description:
This module exploits a stack-based buffer overflow in the Hewlett-Packard
          OmniInet NT Service. By sending a specially crafted MSG_PROTOCOL (0x010b)
          packet, a remote attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code with elevated
          privileges.

          This service is installed with HP OpenView Data Protector, HP Application
          Recovery Manager and potentially other products. This exploit has been tested
          against versions 6.1, 6.0, and 5.50 of Data Protector. and versions 6.0 and 6.1
          of Application Recovery Manager.

          NOTE: There are actually two consecutive wcscpy() calls in the program (which
          may be why ZDI considered them two separate issues). However, this module only
          exploits the second one.
exploits/windows/misc/hp_omniinet_2.reason: Targets HP OmniInet service not present on standard Windows VMs

exploits/windows/misc/hp_omniinet_3.name: HP OmniInet.exe Opcode 27 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/hp_omniinet_3.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in the Hewlett-Packard
          OmniInet NT Service. By sending a specially crafted opcode 27 packet,
          a remote attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code.
targets: HP Data Protector A.06.10 Build 611 / A.06.11 Build 243
exploits/windows/misc/hp_omniinet_3.reason: Targets HP Data Protector, not present on standard Windows VMs

exploits/windows/misc/hp_omniinet_4.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in HP Data Protector's OmniInet
          process.  By supplying a long string of data as the file path with opcode '20',
          a buffer overflow can occur when this data is being written on the stack where
          no proper bounds checking is done beforehand, which results arbitrary code
          execution under the context of SYSTEM.  This module is also made against systems
          such as Windows Server 2003 or Windows Server 2008 that have DEP and/or ASLR
          enabled by default.
targets: HP Data Protector A.06.10 b611 / A.06.11 b243 XP SP3/Win2003/Win2008
exploits/windows/misc/hp_omniinet_4.reason: Targets HP Data Protector OmniInet service, not present on standard Windows VMs

exploits/windows/misc/hp_operations_agen.name: HP Operations Agent Opcode coda.exe 0x34 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/hp_operations_agen.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow vulnerability in HP Operations Agent for
        Windows. The vulnerability exists in the HP Software Performance Core Program
        component (coda.exe) when parsing requests for the 0x34 opcode. This module has
        been tested successfully on HP Operations Agent 11.00 over Windows XP SP3 and
        Windows 2003 SP2 (DEP bypass).

        The coda.exe components runs only for localhost by default, network access must be
        granted through its configuration to be remotely exploitable. On the other hand it
        runs on a random TCP port, to make easier reconnaissance a check function is
        provided.
targets: HP Operations Agent 11.00 / Windows XP SP3
exploits/windows/misc/hp_operations_agen.reason: Service binds only to localhost by default, so no remote network attack surface in a standard VM lab.

exploits/windows/misc/hp_operations_agen.name: HP Operations Agent Opcode coda.exe 0x8c Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/hp_operations_agen.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow vulnerability in HP Operations Agent for
        Windows. The vulnerability exists in the HP Software Performance Core Program
        component (coda.exe) when parsing requests for the 0x8c opcode. This module has
        been tested successfully on HP Operations Agent 11.00 over Windows XP SP3 and
        Windows 2003 SP2 (DEP bypass).

        The coda.exe components runs only for localhost by default, network access must be
        granted through its configuration to be remotely exploitable. On the other hand it
        runs on a random TCP port, to make easier reconnaissance a check function is
        provided.
targets: HP Operations Agent 11.00 / Windows XP SP3
exploits/windows/misc/hp_operations_agen.reason: Service binds only to localhost by default, so no remote network attack surface.

exploits/windows/misc/hp_ovtrace.name: HP OpenView Operations OVTrace Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/hp_ovtrace.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in HP OpenView Operations version A.07.50.
          By sending a specially crafted packet, a remote attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/misc/hp_ovtrace.reason: Targets HP OpenView Operations, not present on standard Windows VMs

exploits/windows/misc/hta_server.name: HTA Web Server
exploits/windows/misc/hta_server.description:
This module hosts an HTML Application (HTA) that when opened will run a
          payload via Powershell. When a user navigates to the HTA file they will
          be prompted by IE twice before the payload is executed.
exploits/windows/misc/hta_server.reason: Requires victim to open HTA file in browser and click through prompts.

exploits/windows/misc/ib_isc_attach_data.name: Borland InterBase isc_attach_database() Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/ib_isc_attach_data.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Borland InterBase
          by sending a specially crafted attach request.
exploits/windows/misc/ib_isc_attach_data.reason: Targets Borland InterBase, a third-party database server not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/ib_isc_create_data.name: Borland InterBase isc_create_database() Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/ib_isc_create_data.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Borland InterBase
          by sending a specially crafted create request.
exploits/windows/misc/ib_isc_create_data.reason: Targets the Borland InterBase database service, which is not installed by default on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/ib_svc_attach.name: Borland InterBase SVC_attach() Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/ib_svc_attach.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Borland InterBase
          by sending a specially crafted service attach request.
exploits/windows/misc/ib_svc_attach.reason: Targets Borland InterBase, a database rarely installed on standard Windows lab VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/ibm_cognos_tm1adms.name: IBM Cognos tm1admsd.exe Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/ibm_cognos_tm1adms.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in IBM Cognos Analytic Server
          Admin service. The vulnerability exists in the tm1admsd.exe component, due to a
          dangerous copy of user controlled data to the stack, via memcpy, without validating
          the supplied length and data. The module has been tested successfully on IBM Cognos
          Express 9.5 over Windows XP SP3.
exploits/windows/misc/ibm_cognos_tm1adms.reason: Targets IBM Cognos Admin service, not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/ibm_director_cim_d.name: IBM System Director Agent DLL Injection
exploits/windows/misc/ibm_director_cim_d.description:
This module abuses the "wmicimsv" service on IBM System Director Agent 5.20.3
        to accomplish arbitrary DLL injection and execute arbitrary code with SYSTEM
        privileges.

        In order to accomplish remote DLL injection it uses a WebDAV service as disclosed
        by kingcope on December 2012. Because of this, the target host must have the
        WebClient service (WebDAV Mini-Redirector) enabled. It is enabled and automatically
        started by default on Windows XP SP3, but disabled by default on Windows 2003 SP2.
targets: IBM System Director Agent 5.20.3 / Windows with WebClient enabled
exploits/windows/misc/ibm_director_cim_d.reason: Requires IBM System Director Agent (rare third-party software) and WebClient service enabled (disabled by default on modern Windows), making it unsupported in a standard Windows VM lab.

exploits/windows/misc/ibm_tsm_cad_ping.name: IBM Tivoli Storage Manager Express CAD Service Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/ibm_tsm_cad_ping.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the IBM Tivoli Storage Manager Express CAD Service.
          By sending a "ping" packet containing a long string, an attacker can execute arbitrary code.

          NOTE: the dsmcad.exe service must be in a particular state (CadWaitingStatus = 1) in order
          for the vulnerable code to be reached. This state doesn't appear to be reachable when the
          TSM server is not running. This service does not restart.
exploits/windows/misc/ibm_tsm_cad_ping.reason: Requires IBM TSM Express CAD service in a specific non-default state and third-party software not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/ibm_tsm_rca_dicuge.name: IBM Tivoli Storage Manager Express RCA Service Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/ibm_tsm_rca_dicuge.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the IBM Tivoli Storage Manager Express Remote
          Client Agent service. By sending a "dicuGetIdentify" request packet containing a long
          NodeName parameter, an attacker can execute arbitrary code.

          NOTE: this exploit first connects to the CAD service to start the RCA service and obtain
          the port number on which it runs. This service does not restart.
exploits/windows/misc/ibm_tsm_rca_dicuge.reason: Exploits a network service (IBM TSM RCA) reachable over TCP, giving remote code execution without credentials or user interaction.

exploits/windows/misc/ibm_websphere_java.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability in IBM's WebSphere Application Server. An unsafe deserialization
          call of unauthenticated Java objects exists to the Apache Commons Collections (ACC) library, which allows
          remote arbitrary code execution. Authentication is not required in order to exploit this vulnerability.
exploits/windows/misc/ibm_websphere_java.reason: Targets a network service (WebSphere) on Windows, needs no credentials or user interaction.

exploits/windows/misc/ivanti_agent_porta.name: Ivanti EPM Agent Portal Command Execution
exploits/windows/misc/ivanti_agent_porta.description:
This module leverages an unauthenticated RCE in Ivanti's EPM Agent Portal where a RPC client can invoke a method
          which will run an attacker-specified string on the remote target as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM.
          This vulnerability is present in versions prior to EPM 2021.1 Su4 and EPM 2022 Su2.
exploits/windows/misc/ivanti_agent_porta.reason: Exploits a network service (Ivanti EPM Agent Portal RPC) for unauthenticated RCE as SYSTEM with no user interaction.

exploits/windows/misc/ivanti_avalanche_m.name: Ivanti Avalanche MDM Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/ivanti_avalanche_m.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow condition in Ivanti Avalanche MDM versions before v6.4.1.
          An attacker can send a specially crafted message to the Wavelink Avalanche Manager,
          which could result in arbitrary code execution with the NT/AUTHORITY SYSTEM permissions.
          This vulnerability occurs during the processing of 3/5/8/100/101/102 item data types.
          The program tries to copy the item data using `qmemcopy` to a fixed size data buffer on stack.
          Upon successful exploitation the attacker gains full access to the target system.

          This vulnerability has been tested against Ivanti Avalanche MDM v6.4.0.0 on Windows 10.
exploits/windows/misc/ivanti_avalanche_m.reason: Targets Ivanti Avalanche MDM, a third-party enterprise MDM product not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/landesk_aolnsrvr.name: LANDesk Management Suite 8.7 Alert Service Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/landesk_aolnsrvr.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in LANDesk Management Suite 8.7. By sending
          an overly long string to the Alert Service, a buffer is overwritten and arbitrary
          code can be executed.
exploits/windows/misc/landesk_aolnsrvr.reason: Targets a third-party management agent not present on standard Windows lab VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/lianja_db_net.name: Lianja SQL 1.0.0RC5.1 db_netserver Stack Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/lianja_db_net.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the db_netserver process, which
          is spawned by the Lianja SQL server.  The issue is fixed in Lianja SQL 1.0.0RC5.2.
exploits/windows/misc/lianja_db_net.reason: Targets the uncommon Lianja SQL db_netserver service not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/manageengine_event.name: ManageEngine EventLog Analyzer Remote Code Execution
exploits/windows/misc/manageengine_event.description:
This module exploits a SQL query functionality in ManageEngine EventLog Analyzer v10.6
          build 10060 and previous versions. Every authenticated user, including the default "guest"
          account can execute SQL queries directly on the underlying Postgres database server. The
          queries are executed as the "postgres" user which has full privileges and thus is able to
          write files to disk. This way a JSP payload can be uploaded and executed with SYSTEM
          privileges on the web server. This module has been tested successfully on ManageEngine
          EventLog Analyzer 10.0 (build 10003) over Windows 7 SP1.
exploits/windows/misc/manageengine_event.reason: Requires authentication (even default 'guest' account) so not an initial foothold without credentials.

exploits/windows/misc/mercury_phonebook.name: Mercury/32 PH Server Module Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/mercury_phonebook.description:
This module exploits a stack-based buffer overflow in
          Mercury/32 <= v4.01b PH Server Module. This issue is
          due to a failure of the application to properly bounds check
          user-supplied data prior to copying it to a fixed size memory buffer.
exploits/windows/misc/mercury_phonebook.reason: Targets Mercury/32 mail server, not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/mini_stream.name: Mini-Stream 3.0.1.1 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/mini_stream.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Mini-Stream 3.0.1.1
          By creating a specially crafted pls file, an attacker may be able
          to execute arbitrary code.
exploits/windows/misc/mini_stream.reason: Exploits a client-side playlist file, requiring the victim to open the malicious .pls file.

exploits/windows/misc/mirc_privmsg_serve.name: mIRC PRIVMSG Handling Stack Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/mirc_privmsg_serve.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in the mIRC IRC Client v6.34 and earlier.
          By enticing a mIRC user to connect to this server module, an excessively long PRIVMSG
          command can be sent, overwriting the stack. Due to size restrictions, ordinal payloads
          may be necessary. This module is based on the code by SkD.
exploits/windows/misc/mirc_privmsg_serve.reason: Victim must connect their mIRC client to the attacker server, requiring user interaction.

exploits/windows/misc/ms07_064_sami.name: MS07-064 Microsoft DirectX DirectShow SAMI Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/ms07_064_sami.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the DirectShow Synchronized
          Accessible Media Interchanged (SAMI) parser in quartz.dll. This module
          has only been tested with Windows Media Player (6.4.09.1129) and
          DirectX 8.0.
exploits/windows/misc/ms07_064_sami.reason: Requires victim to open a malicious SAMI file in Windows Media Player, so it is client-side.

exploits/windows/misc/netcat110_nt.name: Netcat v1.10 NT Stack Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/netcat110_nt.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Netcat v1.10 NT. By sending
          an overly long string we are able to overwrite SEH. The vulnerability
          exists when netcat is used to bind (-e) an executable to a port in doexec.c.
          This module tested successfully using "c:\>nc -L -p 31337 -e ftp".
exploits/windows/misc/netcat110_nt.reason: Targets the standalone netcat binary, not a default Windows service, and requires the victim to manually run nc -L -p <port> -e <exe> first.

exploits/windows/misc/nettransport.name: NetTransport Download Manager 2.90.510 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/nettransport.description:
This exploits a stack buffer overflow in NetTransport Download Manager,
          part of the NetXfer suite. This module was tested
          successfully against version 2.90.510.
exploits/windows/misc/nettransport.reason: Targets third-party download-manager software not present on a standard Windows VM.

exploits/windows/misc/nvidia_mental_ray.name: Nvidia Mental Ray Satellite Service Arbitrary DLL Injection
exploits/windows/misc/nvidia_mental_ray.description:
The Nvidia Mental Ray Satellite Service listens for control commands on port 7414.
          When it receives the command to load a DLL (via an UNC path) it will try to
          connect back to the host on port 7514. If a TCP connection is successful it will
          then attempt to load the DLL. This module has been tested successfully on Win7 x64
          with Nvidia Mental Ray Satellite Service v3.11.1.
exploits/windows/misc/nvidia_mental_ray.reason: Requires third-party Nvidia Mental Ray software not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/plugx.name: PlugX Controller Stack Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/plugx.description: This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the PlugX Controller (C2 server).
exploits/windows/misc/plugx.reason: Exploits the C2 server component, not a Windows service, so it cannot give initial foothold on a Windows VM.

exploits/windows/misc/poppeeper_date.name: POP Peeper v3.4 DATE Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/poppeeper_date.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in POP Peeper v3.4.
          When a specially crafted DATE string is sent to a client,
          an attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code. This
          module is based off of krakowlabs code.
exploits/windows/misc/poppeeper_date.reason: Targets a third-party email client not present on standard Windows VMs and requires victim to retrieve malicious email.

exploits/windows/misc/poppeeper_uidl.name: POP Peeper v3.4 UIDL Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/poppeeper_uidl.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in POP Peeper v3.4.
          When a specially crafted UIDL string is sent to a client,
          an attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code. This
          module is based off of krakowlabs code.
exploits/windows/misc/poppeeper_uidl.reason: Exploits a client-side email reader, requiring the victim to connect to a malicious POP3 server and retrieve the UIDL list.

exploits/windows/misc/realtek_playlist.name: Realtek Media Player Playlist Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/realtek_playlist.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Realtek Media Player(RtlRack) A4.06.
          When a Realtek Media Player client opens a specially crafted playlist, an
          attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code.
targets: Realtek Media Player(RtlRack) A4.06 (XP Pro All English)
exploits/windows/misc/realtek_playlist.reason: Requires the victim to open a malicious playlist file, so it is client-side and needs user interaction.

exploits/windows/misc/sap_2005_license.name: SAP Business One License Manager 2005 Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/sap_2005_license.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the SAP Business One 2005
          License Manager 'NT Naming Service' A and B releases. By sending an
          excessively long string the stack is overwritten enabling arbitrary
          code execution.
exploits/windows/misc/sap_2005_license.reason: SAP Business One is not installed on standard Windows lab VMs

exploits/windows/misc/sap_netweaver_disp.name: SAP NetWeaver Dispatcher DiagTraceR3Info Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/sap_netweaver_disp.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the SAP NetWeaver Dispatcher
          service. The overflow occurs in the DiagTraceR3Info() function and allows a remote
          attacker to execute arbitrary code by supplying a special crafted Diag packet. The
          Dispatcher service is only vulnerable if the Developer Traces have been configured
          at levels 2 or 3. The module has been successfully tested on SAP Netweaver 7.0 EHP2
          SP6 over Windows XP SP3 and Windows 2003 SP2 (DEP bypass).
targets: SAP Netweaver 7.0 EHP2 SP6 / Windows XP SP3
exploits/windows/misc/sap_netweaver_disp.reason: SAP NetWeaver is not installed on standard Windows Server/Desktop VMs

exploits/windows/misc/shixxnote_font.name: ShixxNOTE 6.net Font Field Overflow
exploits/windows/misc/shixxnote_font.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in ShixxNOTE 6.net.
          The vulnerability is caused due to boundary errors in the
          handling of font fields.
exploits/windows/misc/shixxnote_font.reason: Targets the ShixxNOTE 6.net application, which is not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/solarwinds_amqp_de.name: SolarWinds Information Service (SWIS) .NET Deserialization From AMQP RCE
exploits/windows/misc/solarwinds_amqp_de.description:
The SolarWinds Information Service (SWIS) is vulnerable to RCE by way of a crafted message received through the
        AMQP message queue. A malicious user that can authenticate to the AMQP service can publish such a crafted
        message whose body is a serialized .NET object which can lead to OS command execution as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM.
exploits/windows/misc/solarwinds_amqp_de.reason: Requires valid AMQP credentials to publish the malicious message, so no unauthenticated remote foothold.

exploits/windows/misc/solidworks_workgro.name: SolidWorks Workgroup PDM 2014 pdmwService.exe Arbitrary File Write
exploits/windows/misc/solidworks_workgro.description:
This module exploits a remote arbitrary file write vulnerability in
          SolidWorks Workgroup PDM 2014 SP2 and prior.

          For targets running Windows Vista or newer the payload is written to the
          startup folder for all users and executed upon next user logon.

          For targets before Windows Vista code execution can be achieved by first
          uploading the payload as an exe file, and then upload another mof file,
          which schedules WMI to execute the uploaded payload.

          This module has been tested successfully on SolidWorks Workgroup PDM
          2011 SP0 on Windows XP SP3 (EN) and Windows 7 SP1 (EN).
exploits/windows/misc/solidworks_workgro.reason: Targets SolidWorks Workgroup PDM, a third-party CAD service not present on standard Windows VMs.

exploits/windows/misc/splayer_content_ty.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability in SPlayer v3.7 or prior.  When SPlayer
